target.title: fix(agents): classify 'upstream request failed' as server_error for failover
target.body:
## Summary

Provider errors with message `Upstream request failed` (error type `upstream_error`) were not matched by the existing `upstream error` or `upstream connect error` patterns, causing them to fall through to `unclassified` and preventing model fallback from triggering.

Add `upstream request failed` to the server error message patterns.

Fixes #95519.

## Change

`failover-matches.ts`: add `"upstream request failed"` to the `serverError` pattern list alongside existing `"upstream error"` and `"upstream connect error"` entries.

## Tests and validation

| Suite | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `failover-matches.test.ts` | Ō£ģ 23/23 passed |

## Risk

- **Minimal**: one-line addition to an existing pattern list
- The pattern is specific enough to avoid false positives
- All existing server error classification behavior is unchanged

target.title: Add per-agent sandbox session visibility override feature
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandboxed session tools always applied the default sandbox session visibility clamp before considering the requesting agent, so `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` could be configured but was not consistently honored.
- Runtime impact: operators could not safely give one named sandboxed agent full configured session visibility while keeping other sandboxed agents clamped to spawned sessions.
- Change: session visibility resolution now derives the requester agent first, checks that agent's sandbox override, and falls back to `agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` only when no per-agent override exists.
- Compatibility: existing configs keep the same default `"spawned"` clamp unless a named agent explicitly sets `sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility`.

## User-Visible Behavior

New optional per-agent config:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "spawned" }
    },
    "list": [
      { "id": "tony", "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "all" } }
    ]
  }
}
```

For sandboxed session tools, precedence is:

```text
agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
default spawned clamp
```

## Security Impact

- Data access scope can broaden only for an explicitly named sandboxed agent.
- The global/default sandbox behavior remains clamped to spawned sessions.
- Existing `tools.sessions.visibility` and `tools.agentToAgent` policy checks still apply; this does not bypass cross-agent policy.
- No secrets, credentials, network calls, shell execution, or gateway auth behavior changed.

## Files in This PR

- `CHANGELOG.md` - records the user-facing runtime/config behavior change.
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` - documents per-agent sandbox session visibility.
- `docs/gateway/config-tools.md` - documents session tool visibility and sandbox override precedence.
- `docs/tools/multi-agent-sandbox-tools.md` - documents multi-agent sandbox session tool behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts` - wires requester identity into session tool construction.
- `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware sandbox visibility to `session_status`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts` - centralizes requester-aware sandbox visibility resolution.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` - forwards requester context through shared session helper wiring.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_history`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_list`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_send`.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` - covers requester-aware `session_status` behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts` - covers tool-factory wiring for the override.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` - covers end-to-end session tool behavior through the OpenClaw tool factory.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts` - covers access-layer override/fallback resolution.

## Verification

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=360000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - Result: 10 test files passed, 268 tests passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: a sandboxed named agent with `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility="all"` can use the configured `tools.sessions.visibility` scope, while sandboxed agents without that override remain clamped to spawned sessions.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout using production `createOpenClawTools`, embedded OpenClaw gateway session handlers, and real temporary `sessions.json` stores for `main` and `tony`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e <inline proof script>` from the repo root. The script created an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, wrote real per-agent session stores, enabled embedded mode, constructed sandboxed `sessions_list` tools for `main` and `tony`, and compared observed session keys with expected visibility.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "config": {
    "toolsSessionsVisibility": "all",
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] },
    "defaultSandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "spawned",
    "tonySandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "all"
  },
  "observed": {
    "mainSandboxedKeys": ["agent:main:subagent:main-child"],
    "tonySandboxedKeys": [
      "agent:main:main",
      "agent:main:subagent:main-child",
      "agent:tony:main",
      "agent:tony:subagent:tony-child"
    ]
  },
  "result": "PASS"
}
```

Observed result after fix: `main` stayed clamped to spawned sessions through the default sandbox setting; `tony` honored the per-agent `"all"` override and saw the full configured session scope when `tools.agentToAgent` allowed cross-agent access.

What was not tested: remote Crabbox/Testbox behavior. Crabbox is not installed in this shell, and the available Blacksmith CLI is unauthenticated.

## Risk and Rollback

- Risk: an operator may grant broad session visibility to the wrong named agent.
- Mitigation: the override is explicit per agent, default behavior is unchanged, and cross-agent policy remains enforced.
- Rollback: remove `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` from the agent entry to return to the default sandbox clamp.

target.title: feat(slack): add ignoreOtherMentions channel config
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `ignoreOtherMentions` option for Slack channel config, mirroring the existing Discord guild/channel feature
- When enabled, messages that mention another user (`<@U...>`) but do not explicitly mention the bot are dropped ŌĆö even inside bot-participated threads
- This prevents the bot from replying when a user is talking to a coworker in a thread where the bot previously responded

## Slack vs Discord difference

Discord's `ignoreOtherMentions` checks `!wasMentioned && !implicitMention`. The Slack version intentionally omits the `!implicitMention` check because Slack's implicit mention (via `hasSlackThreadParticipation`) fires for **every** message in any bot-participated thread, which would make the feature ineffective.

## Config example

```json5
{
  channels: {
    slack: {
      channels: {
        "*": { ignoreOtherMentions: true },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Changed files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.slack.ts` | Add `ignoreOtherMentions` to `SlackChannelConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` | Add zod validation for the new field |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts` | Wire through type + resolver |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts` | Drop logic before mention gating |

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` passes
- [x] `pnpm test -- extensions/slack/src/monitor` ŌĆö 236 tests pass
- [x] Manual test on live Slack workspace: bot no longer replies when tagging a coworker in a bot thread
- [x] Bot still replies when explicitly @mentioned in the same thread

target.title: fix(cron): stop fallback attempts when cron budget is exhausted
target.body:
## Problem

When a cron job's primary model times out near the cron deadline, the fallback model is started but immediately killed by the same cron timeout ŌĆö receiving effectively 0ms to make an LLM request. The fallback never gets a fair chance to respond, and the user sees a misleading `cron: job execution timed out` error attributed to the fallback model.

**Observed in production logs (2026-03-22):**

```
[08:10:30.733] anthropic/claude-opus-4-6 attempt=1/2 reason=unknown err='Request was aborted.' duration=28667ms
[08:10:30.735] openai-codex/gpt-5.4    attempt=2/2 reason=timeout err='cron: job execution timed out' duration=0ms
```

The cron timeout wraps the entire fallback chain. When the primary model consumes most of the budget, the fallback model is allocated ~0ms and fails immediately.

## Solution

1. **Propagate the cron deadline** (`deadlineAtMs`) from `executeJobCoreWithTimeout` through the cron service state down to `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn`.

2. **Add a `beforeAttempt` hook** to `runWithModelFallback` that is called before each candidate attempt. If it returns `{ type: 'stop' }`, the fallback chain stops and preserves the original error from the previous attempt.

3. **Check remaining budget** in the cron layer's `beforeAttempt` callback. If less than `min(30s, timeout/4)` remains before the cron deadline, the fallback is skipped with a clear message:
   > `Skipping fallback: only 50ms remain before cron timeout (need at least 30000ms).`

This ensures:
- The primary model's real error is preserved (not masked by a fake cron timeout on the fallback)
- No wasted LLM API calls that would be aborted instantly
- The `beforeAttempt` hook is generic and reusable for other pre-attempt checks

## Changes

- `src/agents/model-fallback.ts` ŌĆö Add `beforeAttempt` hook + `stop_before_candidate` decision type
- `src/cron/service/timer.ts` ŌĆö Compute and pass `deadlineAtMs` to job execution
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` ŌĆö Use `beforeAttempt` to check remaining cron budget
- `src/cron/service/state.ts` + `src/gateway/server-cron.ts` ŌĆö Thread `deadlineAtMs` through interfaces

## Tests

- `model-fallback.test.ts` ŌĆö beforeAttempt stop behavior
- `run.fallback-time-budget.test.ts` ŌĆö Cron deadline propagation + fallback budget exhaustion
- `service.issue-regressions.test.ts` ŌĆö Deadline passed to isolated runs


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Cron isolated agent-turn jobs should not start a model fallback when the primary attempt has consumed almost the whole cron deadline and the fallback would be killed immediately by the same timeout.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/cron-fallback-timeout` at `008791fa10`, macOS host, Node 22. The proof command uses the same fallback budget formula added in this branch and the cron job shape from the affected isolated agent-turn surface.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node .artifacts/pr-52365-real-behavior-proof.mjs` locally after the patch, then ran `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.fallback-time-budget.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` as supplemental coverage for the same gate.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the local reproduction command:

```
$ node .artifacts/pr-52365-real-behavior-proof.mjs
cron fallback budget repro
job: sessionTarget=isolated wakeMode=next-heartbeat payload=agentTurn timeoutSeconds=60
primary attempt: consumed 59900ms of a 60000ms cron deadline
fallback check: remainingMs=100; fallbackMinRemainingMs=15000
before(upstream/main): runWithModelFallback had no beforeAttempt budget gate -> fallbackAttempted=true
after(this branch): fallbackAttempted=false
after stop: Skipping fallback: only 100ms remain before cron timeout (need at least 15000ms).
```

Supplemental targeted test output:

```
$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.fallback-time-budget.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
Ō£ō allows fallback attempts when the per-attempt budget fits inside the remaining cron deadline
Ō£ō uses agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds for fallback budget when job timeoutSeconds is unset
Ō£ō stops new fallback attempts when the per-attempt budget no longer fits
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
```

**Observed result after fix:** With only 100ms left before the cron deadline and a 15,000ms minimum fallback budget, the after-fix gate returns `fallbackAttempted=false` and reports `Skipping fallback: only 100ms remain before cron timeout (need at least 15000ms).` The before behavior had no `beforeAttempt` budget gate, so the fallback candidate was started even though it could only receive about 0ms.

**What was not tested:** Live Anthropic/OpenAI API calls were not run for this proof; the local reproduction covers the cron fallback budget decision and the targeted test covers the runtime hook path with mocked provider execution.

target.title: Agents: add machine-readable session status time
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `session_status` only exposed human-readable local time, so reminder scheduling could not reliably compute exact timestamps.
- Why it matters: cron/reminder flows need machine-readable current time to convert requests like "in 30 minutes" into exact ISO-8601 schedules.
- What changed: added a local ISO timestamp formatter, appended local ISO + UTC time to the `session_status` time line, and added a regression test.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no system prompt injection changes, no cron scheduler behavior changes, and no auth/security surface changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #10841
- Related #7650
- Related #9899
- Related #10071

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`session_status` now includes machine-readable current time alongside the readable local time, for example local ISO plus UTC.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4
- Runtime/container: local zsh shell
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): agent `session_status`
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.userTimezone=America/Los_Angeles`, `agents.defaults.timeFormat=12`

### Steps

1. Call `session_status` for a session with a configured user timezone.
2. Inspect the `­¤ĢÆ Time:` line in the returned status card.
3. Confirm it includes readable local time plus local ISO time and UTC time.

### Expected

- `session_status` returns exact machine-readable current time that an agent can use for reminder scheduling.

### Actual

- Before: only human-readable local time.
- After: human-readable local time plus `local ISO ...` and `UTC ...`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: checked the formatter output for `2026-02-08T03:15:00Z` and confirmed it renders `Saturday, February 7th, 2026 ŌĆö 7:15 PM`, `2026-02-07T19:15:00-08:00`, and `2026-02-08T03:15:00Z`.
- Edge cases checked: local offset formatting for a non-UTC timezone; UTC seconds formatting.
- What you did **not** verify: in this Codex terminal environment, `pnpm test -- src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` and raw `vitest run` did not exit cleanly, so I did not get a clean local Vitest completion signal even though the regression test was added.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commit `9bc10cd21`
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/date-time.ts`, `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: malformed time line formatting or missing timezone offset in `session_status`

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `Intl.DateTimeFormat(..., { timeZoneName: "longOffset" })` could behave differently on some runtimes.
  - Mitigation: formatter falls back to existing human-readable time when local ISO formatting fails, and UTC output remains present.

target.title: fix(nextcloud-talk): parse rich object file shares from webhook payload
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #29152

When a user shares a file or image in Nextcloud Talk, the bot webhook receives the message but the NC Talk plugin was not parsing the rich object parameters ŌĆö so the agent never saw the attachment.

NC Talk encodes file shares as rich object messages where `object.content` is a JSON string:
```json
{"message":"{file}","parameters":{"file":{"type":"file","id":"12345","name":"IMG_123.jpg","path":"Talk/IMG_123.jpg","link":"https://cloud.example.com/f/12345","mimetype":"image/jpeg","preview-available":"yes"}}}
```

Previously `payloadToInboundMessage()` passed this raw JSON string as `text`, leaving `mediaUrls` unpopulated.

## Changes

### `monitor.ts`
- Adds `parseRichObjectContent()` helper that detects JSON-encoded rich object payloads
- Extracts file links into `mediaUrls` (https/http only ŌĆö rejects `file://`, `javascript:`, etc.)
- Resolves `{placeholder}` names for human-readable `displayText` (covers file shares, mentions, deck cards, polls, etc.)
- Uses `replaceAll` for placeholder substitution to handle duplicate occurrences
- Falls back to raw `content` for non-rich-object text ŌĆö no behaviour change for regular messages

### `types.ts`
- Adds `mediaUrls?: string[]` to `NextcloudTalkInboundMessage`

### `inbound.ts`
- Passes `MediaUrls` into `finalizeInboundContext` (verified: already consumed natively as `string[]`)
- Relaxes the empty-body guard to allow media-only messages through (previously silently dropped)

## Tests

- `monitor.attachment.test.ts` ŌĆö 7 unit tests: file share, text passthrough, malformed JSON fallback, name fallback, mention resolution, `file://` URL rejection, multi-file
- `monitor.live-integration.test.ts` ŌĆö 5 integration tests using real HMAC-signed payloads against the webhook server

All 12 tests pass. TypeScript compiles clean (`tsc --noEmit`).

## Before / After

**Before:** Sharing a file in NC Talk ŌåÆ agent receives raw JSON string, never sees the attachment.

**After:** Sharing a file ŌåÆ agent receives `text = "filename.jpg"` and `mediaUrls = ["https://..."]` ŌĆö can immediately process the image via vision without any manual workaround.

target.title: test(gateway): consolidate deriveSessionTitle tests into dedicated module
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes an issue where heartbeat polls could overwrite explicit user-labeled session titles with transcript-derived titles like `"heartbeat"`.

## Changes

- Extracts `deriveSessionTitle` to `session-title.ts` with a dedicated test module.
- Preserves explicit session labels before transcript-derived fallbacks.
- Keeps auto-populated `origin.label` behind `firstUserMessage` so unlabeled inbound DMs can still use transcript-derived titles.
- Keeps `session-utils.ts` as a compatibility re-export site.
- Clarifies the zero timestamp test name to describe the actual `updatedAt: 0` input.

## Title precedence

1. `displayName`
2. `subject`
3. explicit `entry.label`
4. `firstUserMessage` (truncated)
5. auto-populated `entry.origin?.label`
6. `sessionId` prefix fallback

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: explicit session labels should survive heartbeat-style transcript summaries, while auto-populated origin labels should not override a real first user message.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on this branch after rebasing onto current `main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --eval 'import { deriveSessionTitle } from "./src/gateway/session-title.ts";
const cases = {
  explicitLabelBeatsHeartbeat: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), label: "My App - Dashboard" }, "heartbeat"),
  transcriptBeatsAutoOriginLabel: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), origin: { label: "Discord: Engineering" } }, "Need help with deployment"),
  originLabelFallbackWithoutTranscript: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abc123", updatedAt: Date.now(), origin: { label: "Discord: Engineering" } }, null),
  zeroUpdatedAtFallback: deriveSessionTitle({ sessionId: "abcd1234-5678-90ef-ghij-klmnopqrstuv", updatedAt: 0 }),
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(cases, null, 2));'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "explicitLabelBeatsHeartbeat": "My App - Dashboard",
  "transcriptBeatsAutoOriginLabel": "Need help with deployment",
  "originLabelFallbackWithoutTranscript": "Discord: Engineering",
  "zeroUpdatedAtFallback": "abcd1234"
}
```

Observed result after fix: explicit labels win over heartbeat transcript text; transcript text wins over auto-populated origin labels; origin labels still act as fallback when there is no transcript; zero `updatedAt` keeps the session-id-only fallback.

What was not tested: full gateway UI rendering was not manually opened; this proof covers the extracted title derivation behavior directly.

## Validation

- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-title.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`

target.title: doctor(config): auto-prune removed google-antigravity-auth entries
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2ŌĆō5 bullets:

- Problem: users can keep stale `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` config after the plugin was removed, which keeps emitting warnings (`#27371`).
- Why it matters: users must manually edit config after updates; startup/doctor warnings stay noisy until they do.
- What changed: doctor compatibility normalization now prunes stale removed plugin references from `plugins.entries`, `plugins.installs`, `plugins.allow`, `plugins.load.paths`, and `plugins.slots.memory`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider runtime/auth behavior changes; this only cleans stale config references.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #27371
- Related #13141

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw doctor --fix` now automatically removes stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin config references instead of requiring manual cleanup.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `plugins.entries["google-antigravity-auth"]` present

### Steps

1. Add stale `google-antigravity-auth` plugin references under `plugins.*`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor --fix`.
3. Re-check config and doctor output.

### Expected

- Stale removed plugin references are pruned automatically.

### Actual

- Confirmed via migration test coverage.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran `pnpm test src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts` and verified new migration test passes.
- Edge cases checked: removal covers entries/installs/allow/load path/memory slot in one pass.
- What you did **not** verify: full end-to-end doctor run on a live upgraded host.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unintended plugin config pruning outside removed plugin IDs.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: accidental over-pruning of plugin config if removal targeting expands incorrectly.
  - Mitigation: migration is hard-scoped to `google-antigravity-auth` and covered by targeted tests.

AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: fix(agents): classify provider upstream_error as fallbackable transient failure
target.body:
## Summary

- Classify provider `upstream_error` / "Upstream request failed" responses as a transient server failure so the configured fallback chain is attempted.
- User-facing impact: a flaky primary provider that returns `upstream_error` now fails over to the next configured model instead of ending the turn with `LLM request failed`.

## Motivation

Closes #95519.

When the primary provider returns an error payload shaped like:

```json
{ "error": { "message": "Upstream request failed", "type": "upstream_error", "param": "", "code": null } }
```

the failover classifier returned `null` (unclassified). Unclassified failures do not enter the model-fallback path, so the run ended with `stopReason: "error"` and the user-facing text `LLM request failed`, with no `model.fallback_step` recorded ŌĆö even when `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks` was configured.

The `serverError` matcher list already covered `"upstream error"` and `"upstream connect error"`, but **not** the `upstream_error` type token nor the `"Upstream request failed"` message phrasing emitted by this provider. So this transient upstream failure slipped through.

The fix adds `"upstream_error"` and `"upstream request failed"` to the `serverError` patterns. These classify as a transient `server_error` (mapped to the `timeout` failover reason), which is the existing fallbackable transient path.

OpenClaw's own generic assistant text `"LLM request failed"` (`GENERIC_ASSISTANT_ERROR_TEXT`) is deliberately left unclassified to avoid a fallback loop on internal/non-provider failures.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts` ŌĆö 30 passed (5 new)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts` ŌĆö 234 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts` ŌĆö 121 passed
- Did NOT change: the generic `"LLM request failed"` text classification (stays `null`).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** the exact `{type:"upstream_error", message:"Upstream request failed"}` payload from the issue was classified `null` ŌåÆ no fallback. After the fix it classifies as `timeout` (fallbackable transient).
- **Real environment:** Node v25.x, vitest 4.1.7, this branch.
- **Exact commands run after the patch:**
  ```
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run <temp test calling classifyFailoverReason on the payloads>
  ```
- **Captured output AFTER fix:**
  ```
  REASON payload = "timeout"
  REASON bare    = "timeout"
  REASON generic = null
  ```
  (`payload` = full issue JSON; `bare` = "Upstream request failed"; `generic` = OpenClaw's own "LLM request failed", correctly left unclassified.)
- **Before the fix**, the same three inputs all returned `null` (verified on `upstream/main` before editing).
- **Regression test:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.test.ts` ŌåÆ `describe("upstream provider transient errors (#95519)")`. It asserts the new classifications AND that the generic text stays `null`; it fails without the source change.
- **What was NOT tested:** end-to-end gateway fallback rotation (covered indirectly by the existing `model-fallback.test.ts` reasonŌåÆcandidate-rotation tests, which still pass); this PR is scoped to the classification gap that gates entry into that path.
target.author: bartok9

commands.author: snowzlmbot

target.title: feat(telegram): unify progress fallback with assistant preview lane
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95004.

Telegram `progress` streaming currently has two related gaps:

- default Telegram progress streaming can appear stuck when upstream answer activity exists but no tool/progress row is available yet;
- users who want full assistant partial previews while keeping the existing progress/tool draft need an explicit opt-in path rather than replacing progress mode with `partial`/`block` behavior.

This PR is a canonical combined proposal that keeps the low-noise default fix from #95183 and incorporates the optional assistant-preview lane from #82303.

## Relationship to Prior PRs / Attribution

This PR intentionally combines two existing approaches:

- #95183 by @snowzlmbot: bounded default Telegram silence fix via short-preview materialization and label-only progress placeholders.
- #82303 by @Fuma2013: opt-in `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` support that keeps the progress/tool draft and streams assistant partial text on a separate assistant preview lane.

Credit for the assistant-preview lane design, config surface, and original regression coverage goes to @Fuma2013 in #82303. This branch cherry-picks those commits and preserves their author attribution, then resolves the interaction with #95183's richer progress draft behavior.

## Summary

- Keep the default Telegram `progress` behavior low-noise:
  - no assistant answer text is streamed into the progress draft by default;
  - upstream answer activity can still materialize a stable label-only progress placeholder so Telegram does not look stuck.
- Keep bounded short-preview behavior for non-progress Telegram drafts:
  - short first previews still respect the debounce;
  - if the turn stays active past the bounded delay, the draft materializes instead of remaining invisible forever.
- Add opt-in Telegram assistant preview lane:
  - `streaming.progress.assistantPreview: true` preserves the existing progress/tool draft;
  - assistant partial text streams on a separate transient assistant lane;
  - the assistant preview lane is cleared/reset before final answer delivery.
- Preserve #95183's rich progress draft rendering while adapting #82303's assistant-preview regression to the HTML progress preview shape.

## Behavior Matrix

- `streaming.mode: "progress"` only:
  - progress/tool/status draft remains the visible progress surface;
  - answer activity can show a label-only placeholder after the bounded delay;
  - assistant answer text is not streamed into the progress draft.
- `streaming.mode: "progress", streaming.progress.assistantPreview: true`:
  - progress/tool/status draft remains intact;
  - assistant partial text uses a separate transient assistant preview lane;
  - final answer delivery clears the assistant preview lane.
- non-progress answer previews:
  - short initial previews are still debounced, but silence is bounded by `minInitialDelayMs`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram progress streaming should no longer look silent by default when answer activity exists without a tool/progress row, and opt-in `streaming.progress.assistantPreview: true` should keep the progress/tool draft while rendering assistant partial text on a separate transient assistant preview lane.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux local OpenClaw checkout on combined PR head `32961074010101826865b3180934db2c8333d8a1`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:schema:check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts \
  src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from the exact-head local run showed:

```text
[base-config-schema] ok
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 41.02s

extension-telegram: bot-message-dispatch.test.ts ŌĆö 131 passed
extension-telegram: draft-stream.test.ts ŌĆö 47 passed
extension-telegram: lane-delivery.test.ts ŌĆö 41 passed
channels: progress-draft-compositor.test.ts ŌĆö 15 passed
plugin-sdk: channel-streaming.test.ts ŌĆö 30 passed
unit-fast: model-runtime-policy.test.ts ŌĆö 13 passed

Total targeted tests: 277 passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix**: the combined candidate preserves the default progress draft as a stable rich Telegram progress surface while preventing assistant answer text from entering that default progress draft; when `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` is enabled, assistant partial text is observed on the separate assistant lane and the progress draft remains intact. The generated channel metadata and base config schema also validate for the new config surface.
- **What was not tested**: live Telegram Bot API network delivery was not re-run on #95522 after the metadata-only identity rewrite; GitHub Actions is still validating the exact head.

## Generated metadata / upgrade compatibility proof

The only stored-data-model warning from ClawSweeper is for `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`. This PR does not add or migrate persisted runtime data. The generated metadata change exposes the new optional Telegram config key `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` so config UIs/schema consumers can discover it. Existing configs remain compatible because the field is optional and defaults to disabled behavior.

Compatibility checks run on the combined head:

```bash
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:schema:check
```

Result: both checks passed after regenerating `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`; no migration step is required.

## Validation

Local targeted validation on this combined branch:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:schema:check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts \
  src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts
```

Result:

- config channel metadata check passed;
- base config schema check passed;
- targeted Vitest shards passed: 277 tests.

## Risk / Review Notes

- This is intentionally larger than #95183 alone because it also includes the opt-in feature surface from #82303.
- The default behavior remains conservative: no assistant text leaks into the default progress draft.
- The opt-in assistant preview lane changes visible Telegram behavior only when `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` is enabled.
- This PR should be treated as the combined/canonical alternative if maintainers prefer one PR over reviewing #95183 and #82303 separately.

target.title: fix(byteplus): add missing cacheRead/cacheWrite pricing for BytePlus models
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Several BytePlus/Doubao models in `extensions/byteplus/openclaw.plugin.json` had `cacheRead: 0` and `cacheWrite: 0` in their cost config, causing context caching costs to be underreported. This fix sets cache pricing for affected models to match the Volcano Engine ARK API caching pricing model.

Affected models: `seed-1-8-251228`, `kimi-k2-5-260127`, `glm-4-7-251222`, `ark-code-latest`, `doubao-seed-code`, `glm-4.7`, `kimi-k2-thinking`, `kimi-k2.5` and their `byteplus-plan` variants.

Fixes #54157

## Why This Change Was Made

Volcano Engine ARK API charges for context caching at a rate of 25% of input price for cache reads and equal to input price for cache writes. The previous config of `cacheRead: 0` / `cacheWrite: 0` meant these costs were not being tracked, leading to inaccurate cost reporting for users of these models.

## User Impact

Cost tracking is now more accurate for BytePlus/Doubao model users ŌĆö context cache reads and writes are properly accounted. No runtime behavior, model selection, or API call behavior changes.

## Evidence

- **Test run**: `pnpm test extensions/byteplus --reporter=verbose`
  - **2 test files passed, 14 tests passed** (1 live test skipped)
  - Key test: `augments the catalog with bundled standard and plan models` ŌĆö confirms model catalog loads correctly with updated pricing
  - All existing plugin manifest, video generation, and catalog tests pass

```text
 Test Files  2 passed | 1 skipped (3)
      Tests  14 passed | 1 skipped (15)
```

- **Catalog validation**: Model catalog test (`augments the catalog with bundled standard and plan models`) loads the full plugin manifest and validates provider model entries ŌĆö no errors with updated cache pricing
- **Pricing formula verification**: `cacheRead: 2.5e-05` = 25% of `input: 0.0001` Ō£ģ; `cacheWrite: 0.0001` = `input: 0.0001` Ō£ģ ŌĆö matches Volcano Engine ARK API documented caching rates

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: BytePlus/Doubao models had `cacheRead: 0` and `cacheWrite: 0` in their cost config, causing context caching costs to be underreported. Eight models across `byteplus` and `byteplus-plan` providers were affected.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 22.19, Linux
- **Test framework**: Vitest
- **Affected file**: `extensions/byteplus/openclaw.plugin.json` (config change only)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
pnpm test extensions/byteplus --reporter=verbose
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
 Test Files  2 passed | 1 skipped (3)
      Tests  14 passed | 1 skipped (15)
```

Key test `augments the catalog with bundled standard and plan models` confirms the plugin manifest loads without errors and all model cost entries (including updated cache pricing) validate correctly through the catalog pipeline.

**Observed result after the fix**: All 14 BytePlus tests pass. The model catalog test validates that cache pricing is correctly parsed from the manifest ŌĆö cache reads at 25% of input price, cache writes at 100% of input price, matching the Volcano Engine ARK API documented caching rate.

**What was not tested**: Live Volcano Engine ARK API billing verification ŌĆö the actual cost calculation on cached requests is tested indirectly through unit tests that validate the pricing model values. End-to-end billing accuracy requires live API usage with context caching enabled.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`No` ŌĆö cost tracking improvement only)
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

target.title: fix(agents): verify completion delivery target
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens ACP/subagent completion announcement delivery accounting so a completion is only counted as visibly delivered when the delivery evidence matches the requester route.

It:
- propagates outbound payload target metadata into delivery status
- validates automatic completion delivery against provider/account/channel/thread targets
- requires exact account/thread evidence for threaded origins
- preserves target metadata on failed payload outcomes for diagnostics
- keeps the current-chat generated-media compatibility path while avoiding explicit wrong-target matches

## Behavior addressed

Completion announcements can be injected through an internal/inter-session path while the original requester is a channel/thread. Broad checks such as ŌĆ£visible payload existsŌĆØ or ŌĆ£message tool sent somethingŌĆØ can count delivery as successful even when the visible delivery evidence belongs to another route.

This change makes completion delivery proof target-aware: wrong thread, missing thread, missing account, and provider mismatches no longer satisfy delivery for a concrete requester route.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Completion delivery is only counted as visibly delivered when runtime delivery evidence matches the requester route, including concrete account/thread target. Wrong-thread message-tool evidence must be rejected; exact-thread evidence must be accepted.
- **Real setup tested**: Disposable checkout of PR branch `anyech/openclaw:fix/acp-completion-thread-delivery` under `tmp/github-open-work/real-proof-20260603T073207Z/pr-89287`. A one-off non-production runtime harness (`proof-89287.ts`, saved in the review artifact directory and disposable checkout) imported the real `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` module and called `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` through the real announce dispatch/direct-delivery path. Synthetic session store/config/origin data used a fake Discord group/thread route; only the boundary gateway/transport deps were substituted to record and return delivery envelopes. No production Gateway/config/runtime/state or private deployment proof was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Terminal commands run in the disposable checkout were `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts` and `node --import tsx proof-89287.ts`; full command transcript is in `review/real-proof-build-20260603T073207Z/messaging/exact-commands-used.txt`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output / runtime log excerpt from `review/real-proof-build-20260603T073207Z/messaging/evidence/proof-89287.log`:

  ```json
  {
    "agentParams": {"deliver":false,"channel":"discord","to":"channel:proof-parent","threadId":"thread-proof-expected","sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"},
    "wrong": {"delivered":false,"path":"direct","reason":"message_tool_delivery_missing","error":"completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery"},
    "correct": {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: With the same synthetic requester route, message-tool evidence for `thread-proof-wrong` was rejected/flagged as missing target delivery, while message-tool evidence for `thread-proof-expected` was accepted as direct completion delivery. This exercised the repository runtime announce/direct-delivery implementation, not only an isolated matcher function.
- **What was not tested**: No live production Gateway, live Discord workspace, real bot token, or private deployment state was used. The outbound provider and gateway agent call were substituted only at the non-production boundary so the proof remains sanitized.

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(control-ui): clear stale composer replays after send
target.body:
## Summary

- After sending a message, the Control UI composer clears the draft. A stale `input` event can replay the just-submitted text back into the textarea, making it look like the message was never sent (Windows Chrome/Edge, #89466).
- **Root-cause fix (this round):** the stale-replay guard previously only treated **non-`InputEvent`** events as replays (`!(e instanceof InputEvent)`). The real composer replays the submitted value through a **native `InputEvent`**, so it slipped past the guard into `syncComposerValue` and restored the text. The guard now discriminates on the cleared-submitted-draft state (matching text + empty host draft + unchanged `inputVersion`) for **any** event, exempting only deliberate paste/drop insertions and IME composition.
- Deliberate same-text re-entry still works: a paste/drop carries an explicit `inputType`, and fresh typing advances `inputVersion` before the stale check.
- What did **not** change: send path, host draft ownership, IME composition handling, slash-menu behavior.

## Linked context

Closes #89466.
Related #94901 (alternative app-chat-marker approach) ŌĆö thanks for the shared analysis; this PR keeps the composer-boundary guard and adds native-replay coverage + real-browser proof.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** After send clears the Control UI composer, a stale **native `InputEvent`** replay of the submitted text no longer reappears in the textarea, while a deliberate same-text re-entry is still accepted.

**Real environment tested:** Real Chromium (Playwright) driving the Control UI Vite app with the repo's mocked Gateway harness (`ui/src/test-helpers/control-ui-e2e.ts`), on Windows 10 + Node 22, patched branch head `ce16136921ac`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# Real-browser e2e (new): types + sends, replays a native InputEvent, re-enters text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner \
  ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-composer-stale-replay-89466.e2e.test.ts

# Focused unit regression incl. native InputEvent inputType variants
pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Real-browser e2e run (Chromium):

```text
 Ō£ō |ui-e2e| ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-composer-stale-replay-89466.e2e.test.ts (1 test) 
   Ō£ō clears after send, suppresses a stale native InputEvent replay, and accepts re-entry
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```

In-browser captured values: `afterSend = ""`, `afterReplay = ""` (native `InputEvent` with `inputType: "insertText"` replaying the submitted text), `afterReentry = "submitted message"`.

Screenshots captured under `.artifacts/control-ui-e2e/chat-composer-stale-replay-89466/`:
- `01-cleared-after-send.png` ŌĆö composer empty (placeholder shown) after send; "submitted message" appears as a sent chat bubble.
- `02-stale-replay-suppressed.png` ŌĆö after the native `InputEvent` replay, the composer is still empty (placeholder); the submitted text did not reappear.
- `03-same-text-reentry.png` ŌĆö after deliberate keyboard re-entry, the composer shows "submitted message" again.

Unit regression (jsdom), native `InputEvent` path now covered:

```text
$ pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  114 passed (114)
```

**Observed result after fix:** The real browser composer clears after send, ignores the stale **native** replay, and still accepts a deliberate same-text re-entry.

**What was not tested:** A live remote Gateway/provider session. The browser proof uses the repo's deterministic mocked Gateway harness. The exact Chrome/Edge timing quirk that emits the stale replay is reproduced by dispatching the native `InputEvent` the real composer would deliver.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-composer-stale-replay-89466.e2e.test.ts` ŌĆö new real-browser e2e (1 passed).
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` ŌĆö 114 passed, including the new `ignores a stale native InputEvent replay after send cleared the host draft` test (covers `insertText`/`insertReplacementText`/empty `inputType`) plus the existing deliberate-paste and host-prefill cases.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes ŌĆö the composer no longer restores submitted text after send when a native replay fires; deliberate re-entry is unchanged.
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
Highest-risk area (`merge-risk: message-delivery`): the composer state path that decides whether submitted text is cleared/retained/replayed. Mitigation: the guard only suppresses when the text matches the just-submitted draft AND the host draft is empty AND `inputVersion` is unchanged since send; deliberate paste/drop and IME composition are explicitly exempted, and both real-browser + unit tests lock the contract.

## Current review state

Next action: ClawSweeper re-review on head `ce16136921ac`, then maintainer merge.
Waiting on: `proof: sufficient` / `ready for maintainer look`.
Bot comments addressed (June 19 review):
- [P2 / "patch is incorrect"] Native `InputEvent` replays are now suppressed ŌĆö the guard no longer keys off `!(e instanceof InputEvent)`; it uses the cleared-submitted-draft + `inputVersion` discriminator and exempts only deliberate paste/drop and IME.
- [P1 proof] Replaced the generic `Event("input")` evidence with a real-browser e2e that replays a native `InputEvent` and with a unit regression covering native `inputType` variants; pasted run output above.
- [P1 test] Added `ignores a stale native InputEvent replay after send cleared the host draft`.
- Competing-PR note: reviewed #94901; kept the smaller composer-boundary fix and added the native-replay coverage it and this PR both need.

target.title: feat(telegram): unify progress fallback with assistant preview lane
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95004.

Telegram `progress` streaming currently has two related gaps:

- default Telegram progress streaming can appear stuck when upstream answer activity exists but no tool/progress row is available yet;
- users who want full assistant partial previews while keeping the existing progress/tool draft need an explicit opt-in path rather than replacing progress mode with `partial`/`block` behavior.

This PR is a canonical combined proposal that keeps the low-noise default fix from #95183 and incorporates the optional assistant-preview lane from #82303.

## Relationship to Prior PRs / Attribution

This PR intentionally combines two existing approaches:

- #95183 by @snowzlmbot: bounded default Telegram silence fix via short-preview materialization and label-only progress placeholders.
- #82303 by @Fuma2013: opt-in `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` support that keeps the progress/tool draft and streams assistant partial text on a separate assistant preview lane.

Credit for the assistant-preview lane design, config surface, and original regression coverage goes to @Fuma2013 in #82303. This branch cherry-picks those commits and preserves their author attribution, then resolves the interaction with #95183's richer progress draft behavior.

## Summary

- Keep the default Telegram `progress` behavior low-noise:
  - no assistant answer text is streamed into the progress draft by default;
  - upstream answer activity can still materialize a stable label-only progress placeholder so Telegram does not look stuck.
- Keep bounded short-preview behavior for non-progress Telegram drafts:
  - short first previews still respect the debounce;
  - if the turn stays active past the bounded delay, the draft materializes instead of remaining invisible forever.
- Add opt-in Telegram assistant preview lane:
  - `streaming.progress.assistantPreview: true` preserves the existing progress/tool draft;
  - assistant partial text streams on a separate transient assistant lane;
  - the assistant preview lane is cleared/reset before final answer delivery.
- Preserve #95183's rich progress draft rendering while adapting #82303's assistant-preview regression to the HTML progress preview shape.

## Behavior Matrix

- `streaming.mode: "progress"` only:
  - progress/tool/status draft remains the visible progress surface;
  - answer activity can show a label-only placeholder after the bounded delay;
  - assistant answer text is not streamed into the progress draft.
- `streaming.mode: "progress", streaming.progress.assistantPreview: true`:
  - progress/tool/status draft remains intact;
  - assistant partial text uses a separate transient assistant preview lane;
  - final answer delivery clears the assistant preview lane.
- non-progress answer previews:
  - short initial previews are still debounced, but silence is bounded by `minInitialDelayMs`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram progress streaming should no longer look silent by default when answer activity exists without a tool/progress row, and opt-in `streaming.progress.assistantPreview: true` should keep the progress/tool draft while rendering assistant partial text on a separate transient assistant preview lane.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux local OpenClaw checkout on combined PR head `32961074010101826865b3180934db2c8333d8a1`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:schema:check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts \
  src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from the exact-head local run showed:

```text
[base-config-schema] ok
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 41.02s

extension-telegram: bot-message-dispatch.test.ts ŌĆö 131 passed
extension-telegram: draft-stream.test.ts ŌĆö 47 passed
extension-telegram: lane-delivery.test.ts ŌĆö 41 passed
channels: progress-draft-compositor.test.ts ŌĆö 15 passed
plugin-sdk: channel-streaming.test.ts ŌĆö 30 passed
unit-fast: model-runtime-policy.test.ts ŌĆö 13 passed

Total targeted tests: 277 passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix**: the combined candidate preserves the default progress draft as a stable rich Telegram progress surface while preventing assistant answer text from entering that default progress draft; when `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` is enabled, assistant partial text is observed on the separate assistant lane and the progress draft remains intact. The generated channel metadata and base config schema also validate for the new config surface.
- **What was not tested**: live Telegram Bot API network delivery was not re-run on #95522 after the metadata-only identity rewrite; GitHub Actions is still validating the exact head.

## Generated metadata / upgrade compatibility proof

The only stored-data-model warning from ClawSweeper is for `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`. This PR does not add or migrate persisted runtime data. The generated metadata change exposes the new optional Telegram config key `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` so config UIs/schema consumers can discover it. Existing configs remain compatible because the field is optional and defaults to disabled behavior.

Compatibility checks run on the combined head:

```bash
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:schema:check
```

Result: both checks passed after regenerating `src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`; no migration step is required.

## Validation

Local targeted validation on this combined branch:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm config:schema:check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts \
  src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.test.ts \
  src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts \
  src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts
```

Result:

- config channel metadata check passed;
- base config schema check passed;
- targeted Vitest shards passed: 277 tests.

## Risk / Review Notes

- This is intentionally larger than #95183 alone because it also includes the opt-in feature surface from #82303.
- The default behavior remains conservative: no assistant text leaks into the default progress draft.
- The opt-in assistant preview lane changes visible Telegram behavior only when `streaming.progress.assistantPreview` is enabled.
- This PR should be treated as the combined/canonical alternative if maintainers prefer one PR over reviewing #95183 and #82303 separately.

target.title: fix(agents): auto-correct hallucinated docx/pptx/xlsx extensions in tool path params
target.body:
## Summary
Auto-correct model-hallucinated document file extensions (`.docodex` ŌåÆ `.docx`, `.pptcodex` ŌåÆ `.pptx`, `.xlscodex` ŌåÆ `.xlsx`) at the shared agent-tool parameter boundary. The correction is applied to `path` parameters in file tools (read/write/edit) before filesystem execution, alongside the existing XML suffix normalization pipeline.

Fixes #93326

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Model-supplied paths like `report.docodex` pass unchanged through the current path-normalization pipeline, causing file-operation failures. Existing path cleanup only strips malformed XML suffixes (`</arg_value>>`) and does not handle hallucinated document extensions.

**Real setup tested**:

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 ŌĆö group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged ŌĆö the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** ŌĆö no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts ŌĆö a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** ŌĆö only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message ŌĆö the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op ŌĆö
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL ŌĆö session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED ŌĆö terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: feat(gateway): add image.providers RPC
target.body:
## Summary

Add read-only `image.providers` Gateway RPC that exposes image generation provider inventory metadata (id, label, configured, defaultModel, models, capabilities) without credentials or secrets. Follows the same pattern as `tts.providers`.

## Changes

- Add `image.providers` to Gateway core descriptors (operator.read scope)
- Create `imageHandlers` module following `tts.providers` pattern
- Register lazy handler in `server-methods.ts`
- Add protocol schema and validator in `gateway-protocol` package
- Handler validates response against schema before returning to client
- Export schema through `./schema` barrel (per ClawSweeper finding 6)
- Add 4 focused test cases (per ClawSweeper P2 requirement)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Exposes image generation provider inventory metadata over Gateway RPC for control UIs without spawning CLI process.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw setup with image generation providers configured.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
pnpm build
pnpm openclaw gateway call image.providers
```

**Environment metadata:**
- Git commit: ccfb7fd8acf8cdd75e8fba54bc98cd3d75573ccb
- Node: v22.19.0
- OS: Darwin 24.6.0
- OpenClaw: built from local checkout

**Actual JSON output:**
```json
{
  "providers": [
    {
      "id": "openai",
      "label": "OpenAI",
      "configured": true,
      "defaultModel": "dall-e-3",
      "models": ["dall-e-2", "dall-e-3"],
      "capabilities": {
        "generate": { "enabled": true },
        "edit": { "enabled": false },
        "geometry": false,
        "output": ["png"]
      }
    }
  ],
  "active": null
}
```

**Observed result:** Successfully returns provider array. `active` field resolved from `agents.defaults.imageGenerationModel.primary`; returns `null` when not configured. Handler validates response against `ImageProvidersResultSchema` before responding; validation failure returns `INVALID_REQUEST` error code.

## Verification

- `pnpm build` passes
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passes (noUnusedParameters clean)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/image.test.ts` passes (4 test cases)

## Risk assessment

- **merge-risk: auth-provider** ŌĆö This RPC is read-only and does not modify OAuth/token/credential paths. The `configured` field uses `provider.isConfigured()` real readiness semantics (no `startsWith` auth-profile guessing). The `active` field is derived strictly from `agents.defaults.imageGenerationModel.primary` and returns `null` when unset (no fallback invention).

- **merge-risk: compatibility** ŌĆö Pure additive change (+409/-0). No existing RPC modified. Schema is exported through both `./index` (types) and `./schema` (schema barrel). Schema fields aligned with `src/image-generation/types.ts` including `edit.maxInputImages`, `geometry.aspectRatiosByModel`, `geometry.resolutionsByModel`.

## Related

- Fixes #78330

target.title: fix(agents): prevent Codex missing_tool_result from triggering cross-provider model fallback
target.body:
## Summary
When the Codex harness synthesizes a `missing_tool_result` for a hung native `bash` call, the error percolates through the failover classification pipeline without matching any known provider-failure pattern. The outer `runWithModelFallback` loop in `model-fallback.ts` treats unrecognized errors as retryable when remaining model candidates exist (line 1789), causing a cross-provider model fallback that silently switches the user's model mid-conversation. No other provider/model can fix a local tool execution failure.

This fix adds detection of the Codex `MISSING_TOOL_RESULT_ERROR` text pattern in `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError`, which is already checked at both the inner runner (line 366) and outer fallback loop (line 1717) in `model-fallback.ts`. When detected, the error is treated as a non-provider local failure and the fallback chain aborts immediately instead of consuming candidate slots.

Fixes #95474

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Codex `missing_tool_result` (synthetic error for hung native tool calls) triggers cross-provider model fallback, silently switching the user's model mid-conversation to a different provider that also cannot fix the local tool failure.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 22.19, Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64
- **Code analysis path**: See root cause analysis below
- **Test framework**: Vitest 4.1.8

**Root cause**: The outer fallback loop in `model-fallback.ts:1789` assumes all unrecognized (non-FailoverError) errors are retryable when remaining candidates exist. The Codex `MISSING_TOOL_RESULT_ERROR` ("OpenClaw recorded a native Codex tool.call without a matching tool.result before the turn completed.") does not match any failover classification pattern, so `coerceToFailoverError` returns null. The raw error becomes `normalized` at line 1737, `isKnownFailover` is false at line 1792, and the loop continues to the next model candidate ŌĆö even though no other provider can fix a local command.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
cd /home/0668001085/workspace/openclaw-worktrees/fix/issue-95474
node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/failover-error.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts --reporter=verbose
node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/agents/failover-error.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
Ō£ō |unit-fast| failover-error > isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError > returns true for direct Codex missing native tool result errors
Ō£ō |unit-fast| failover-error > isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError > returns true when the missing tool result error is nested via cause
Ō£ō |unit-fast| failover-error > isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError > returns true when the missing tool result error is nested via error property
Ō£ō |unit-fast| failover-error > isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError > returns false for unrelated error messages that mention 'tool'

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  95 passed (95)

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  164 passed (164)  (model-fallback regression suite)

$ pnpm format:check src/agents/failover-error.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 18ms on 2 files using 1 threads.
```

**Observed result after the fix**: `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` now returns `true` for errors containing the Codex `MISSING_TOOL_RESULT_ERROR` pattern (both direct and nested via `cause`/`error` properties). The model fallback chain will abort on these local tool execution failures instead of advancing to the next candidate. All existing tests continue to pass with no regressions.

**What was not tested**: Live end-to-end reproduction with a hung Codex bash call. The fix is validated at the source level with unit tests covering the exact error message shape the Codex harness produces during `synthesizeMissingToolResults` (confirmed by ClawSweeper source-repro review).

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | Ō£ģ | 95/95 passed (4 new + 91 existing) in failover-error.test.ts |
| Regression (model-fallback) | Ō£ģ | 164/164 passed |
| Format (oxfmt) | Ō£ģ | Clean on both changed files |

## Risk checklist
**Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes` - Users encountering hung Codex native tool calls will no longer see their model silently switch to a different provider. The error will surface as a terminal error on the same model, which is the intended behavior.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No`

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No`

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested
