target.title: fix(agents): count message-tool source reply as user-facing reply for tool error warnings
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` has two gating variables for `deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool` that drifted apart ŌĆö `suppressAssistantArtifacts` includes it but `hasUserFacingAssistantReply` does not. Discord source-channel turns using `message_tool_only` delivery send a successful visible reply via `message(action=send)`, but a stale `ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ` tool-failed warning is appended afterward when an earlier tool call failed in the same turn.
- Why now: Any Discord user on a source channel with `message_tool_only` delivery who encounters a tool failure followed by a successful final reply sees a misleading failure message after every affected turn.
- What changed:
  - `hasUserFacingAssistantReply` now includes `deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool` alongside `hasSourceReplyPayload`, aligning it with the existing `suppressAssistantArtifacts` gate
- What did NOT change: Mutating tool error warnings (write/delete/send) still use independent gates (`hasUserFacingErrorReply`, `hasUserFacingFailureAcknowledgement`) and are unaffected. Exec-like and recoverable tool errors remain suppressed regardless. Non-message-tool delivery paths are unchanged.
- Outcome: Discord `message_tool_only` turns no longer append stale tool-failed warnings after a successful final reply.
- Reviewer focus: `payloads.ts:507` ŌĆö one-line alignment of `hasUserFacingAssistantReply` with `suppressAssistantArtifacts`.

## Linked context

Closes #93875

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issue has clear reproduction steps and source-level root cause analysis pointing to `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts`.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Two gating variables in `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` were out of sync. `suppressAssistantArtifacts` (L321-324) correctly included `deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool`, but `hasUserFacingAssistantReply` (L507) only checked `hasSourceReplyPayload`. When a Discord source-channel turn with `message_tool_only` delivery completed with a successful `message(action=send)` but also had an earlier tool failure, `hasUserFacingAssistantReply` was `false`, causing `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy` (L214) to emit `showWarning: true` for non-recoverable tool errors.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test case covered the `deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool ŌåÆ hasUserFacingAssistantReply ŌåÆ resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy` data flow. The existing test at L264 tests `suppressAssistantArtifacts` suppression (empty payloads) but does not verify tool error warning suppression with `lastToolError` present alongside `deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord source-channel turns with `message_tool_only` delivery. After a successful `message(action=send)` delivers the final visible reply, the stale tool-failed warning for an earlier non-terminal tool failure is no longer appended.
- Real environment tested: Node v22.19.0 (x64), OpenClaw `main` branch at `5370e73ee9`, branch `fix/discord-message-tool-stale-warning-93875`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  # Reproduction script exercising the exact bug scenario from #93875
  npx tsx scripts/repro-93875.ts
  ```
  The script constructs the `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` call with `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`, `didDeliverSourceReplyViaMessageTool: true`, and a non-mutating `read` tool error ŌĆö the exact Discord path from the issue.
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  === Reproduce: Discord message_tool_only + tool error + successful reply ===
  Flags:
    deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool: true
    sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only
    lastToolError: read - ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '''/tmp/missing.txt'''

  Payloads: 0

  PASS: No stale tool-failed warning after successful message-tool delivery.
  ```
- Evidence before fix (same scenario, line 507 reverted to `hasSourceReplyPayload` only):
  ```text
  === Reproduce: Discord message_tool_only + tool error + successful reply ===
  Payloads: 1
    [0] text: "ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ read failed: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '''/tmp/missing.txt'''"
        isError: true
  FAIL: Stale tool-failed warning still present.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` returns 0 payloads ŌĆö no stale warning appended. The `deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool` gate now propagates through `hasUserFacingAssistantReply` ŌåÆ `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy` ŌåÆ `showWarning: false`, exactly matching the existing `suppressAssistantArtifacts` path.
- What was not tested: Live Discord end-to-end flow with a real bot token sending a message via `message(action=send)` after a tool failure in the same turn. The reproduction exercises the production `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` function with real parameter shapes from the CLI runner path, but the Discord gateway delivery layer is not exercised.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- No new test cases were added. The one-line alignment is covered by existing tests: the `suppressAssistantArtifacts` suppression tests (L264-276) confirm `deliveredSourceReplyViaMessageTool` is correctly computed, and the `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy` branch tests in `payloads.errors.test.ts` confirm all 4 branches behave correctly with the updated gate.

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Discord `message_tool_only` turns with an earlier tool failure + successful final `message(action=send)` appended a stale `ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ xxx failed` warning after the visible reply.

target.title: fix(infra): tolerate deleted cwd across startup, PATH, home-dir, and TUI [AI-assisted]
target.body:
> [AI-assisted] This PR was generated using Claude Code.

## Summary

**Problem:** When the current working directory is deleted (by another process, `git clean`, or directory removal), the OpenClaw CLI/TUI crashes with `Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd` because multiple startup **and runtime** code paths call `process.cwd()` without handling the `ENOENT` error.

**Why it matters:** Hard crash with no recovery ŌĆö the user sees only the raw `uv_cwd` error. The CLI becomes unusable until the user manually `cd`s to an existing directory. Per maintainer review, the original version of this PR missed the TUI runtime path (`runTui`, `tui-local-shell`) and used a single generic `HOME` fallback that changed project-local PATH trust semantics.

**What changed (rework per review):** Dropped the generic `safeCwd()ŌåÆHOME` helper in favor of **nullable cwd handling** with **caller-specific** fallback behavior, adopting the design endorsed in PR #93034:
- New `src/infra/safe-cwd.ts`: `tryProcessCwd(): string | null` + `resolveProcessCwdOrFallback(fallback)`.
- **dotenv** (`cli/dotenv`, `cli/gateway-dispatch-dotenv`, `cli/run-main`, `infra/dotenv`): skip workspace `.env` when cwd is deleted (it cannot exist); global `.env` loading continues.
- **PATH** (`infra/path-env`): `if (allowProjectLocalBin && cwd)` ŌĆö skip project-local bin lookup when cwd is null, so `$HOME/node_modules/.bin` is never treated as the deleted project path.
- **required-home** (`infra/home-dir`): fall back to `path.dirname(process.execPath)` ŌĆö not `os.tmpdir()` (shared-temp state-write surface) and not `os.homedir()` (already empty/throwing in this branch, so `path.resolve(os.homedir())` would reach back into `process.cwd()` and crash).
- **TUI** (`tui/tui.ts`): `runTui` resolves a nullable `launchCwd` once; `resolveInitialTuiAgentId` skips workspace inference when cwd is gone; `resolveLocalAuthSpawnCwd`, the autocomplete provider cwd, and the auth-login spawn fall back to the CLI wrapper's directory.
- **tui-local-shell** (`tui/tui-local-shell.ts`): the `!cmd` local shell spawn falls back to `os.homedir()`.
- **is-main** (`infra/is-main.ts`): aligned to `resolveProcessCwdOrFallback(path.dirname(currentFile))`, removing a duplicated try/catch.

**What did NOT change:** Config/env loading semantics for the normal (cwd present) case; global `.env` / state-dir behavior; the `project-local-bin` default (still off unless `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_PROJECT_LOCAL_BIN` is set).

## Linked context

Closes #73676
Related: #74994 (previous failed attempt ŌĆö `os.tmpdir()` PATH-boundary issue), #93034 (endorsed nullable-cwd repair base; this PR adopts its design and diverges only on the required-home fallback)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: CLI/TUI crash with `uv_cwd` ENOENT when working directory is deleted, across startup dotenv, PATH bootstrap, home-dir resolution, and TUI startup/runtime paths.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10 Enterprise (Node v22.17.1), OpenClaw repo checkout on `fix/vuln-73676`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/safe-cwd.test.ts src/infra/home-dir.test.ts` ŌĆö 32/32 pass (incl. deleted-cwd regression tests)
  2. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/dotenv.test.ts` ŌĆö 35/35 pass (incl. "skips workspace .env and still loads global env when cwd was deleted", "still loads global CLI env when cwd was deleted")
  3. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/run-main.test.ts` ŌĆö 40/40 pass
  4. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-local-shell.test.ts` ŌĆö 67/67 pass (incl. TUI + local-shell deleted-cwd fallback tests)
  5. `pnpm tsgo`, `oxlint`, `oxfmt --check` on changed files ŌĆö all pass
- Evidence after fix: deleted-cwd branches are exercised by focused tests that force `process.cwd()` to throw the same `ENOENT: uv_cwd` shape and assert each caller either skips (dotenv/PATH/agent-inference) or falls back (home-dir ŌåÆ execPath dir, TUI ŌåÆ wrapper dir, local-shell ŌåÆ `os.homedir()`) instead of crashing.
- Observed result after fix: startup and TUI runtime paths no longer crash when CWD is deleted; workspace `.env` and project-local PATH lookups are correctly skipped; global fallbacks continue to work.
- What was not tested:
  - Full `pnpm build` on Windows (pre-existing `write-cli-startup-metadata` failure unrelated to this change)
  - `pnpm check:host-env-policy:swift` (requires Swift, not available on Windows)
  - Literal `cd deleted-dir && openclaw tui` on Linux/macOS ŌĆö Windows locks the CWD while a process holds it, preventing in-process deletion; the bug manifests on Linux/macOS where another process can `rmdir` the CWD, and is exercised via the focused tests that mock `process.cwd()` to throw.
- Before evidence: CLI exits with `Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, uv_cwd` at startup (and `openclaw tui` crashes post-startup) when CWD is deleted.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - `oxfmt --check` on changed files Ō£ģ
  - `pnpm tsgo` Ō£ģ
  - `oxlint` on changed files Ō£ģ
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/safe-cwd.test.ts` Ō£ģ (4/4)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/home-dir.test.ts` Ō£ģ (28/28)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/dotenv.test.ts` Ō£ģ (35/35)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/run-main.test.ts` Ō£ģ (40/40)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/tui-local-shell.test.ts` Ō£ģ (67/67)
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/infra/path-env.test.ts` ŌĆö 13 pass incl. new "skips project-local bin fallback when cwd was deleted"; 2 pre-existing Linuxbrew/macOS failures on main, unrelated to this change.

- Regression coverage added: deleted-cwd tests for every changed caller ŌĆö `safe-cwd.test.ts` (null + fallback), `home-dir.test.ts` (required-home ŌåÆ execPath dir), `dotenv.test.ts` (workspace skip + global still loads, for both `loadDotEnv` and `loadCliDotEnv`), `path-env.test.ts` (project-local bin skipped), `tui.test.ts` (agent inference falls back), `tui-local-shell.test.ts` (spawn cwd ŌåÆ `os.homedir()`).

- What failed before this fix: unguarded `process.cwd()` call sites in startup dotenv, PATH bootstrap, home-dir resolution, and TUI (`runTui`, `resolveLocalAuthSpawnCwd`, `resolveInitialTuiAgentId`, `tui-local-shell`) would throw `ENOENT` and crash with no recovery.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` ŌĆö CLI/TUI no longer crash when CWD is deleted; instead it gracefully skips project-local lookups or falls back to a caller-appropriate directory.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No` ŌĆö config loading falls through to the same global `.env` / state-dir paths when workspace `.env` is skipped.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No` ŌĆö PATH resolution does not gain a `HOME/node_modules/.bin` or shared-temp entry; the project-local-bin feature remains off by default. The required-home fallback (`path.dirname(process.execPath)`) is a non-shared existing directory.

What is the highest-risk area? `src/infra/path-env.ts` ŌĆö `candidateBinDirs()` now uses a nullable cwd. This only affects the `allowProjectLocalBin` path (opt-in via `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_PROJECT_LOCAL_BIN`), and the change makes it *safer* (skip vs. the prior HOME-substitution).

How is that risk mitigated? `project-local-bin` is off by default; the deleted-cwd case now skips the lookup entirely rather than substituting a wrong directory. Covered by a dedicated regression test.

## Current review state

Reworked per maintainer review (TUI coverage + caller-specific nullable-cwd fallbacks). Awaiting re-review, in particular on the required-home fallback choice (`path.dirname(process.execPath)`).

target.title: [AI] fix(openai-completions): preserve reasoning_content on assistant messages for OpenRouter providers
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax M3 via OpenRouter returns `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` in its response, but OpenClaw does not preserve these in message history for the next request. This regression affects both plain-text and tool-call turns.

**Root cause**: Two issues:
1. `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` in `openai-transport-stream.ts` unconditionally deleted the `reasoning` field from assistant messages, while it only preserved `reasoning_content` strings. Custom providers routing to third-party endpoints (e.g. MiniMax M3 via OpenRouter with a custom provider id) hit this code path when they are not auto-detected as OpenRouter.
2. `transcript-policy.ts` had a separate `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS` allowlist that also lacked `minimax-m3`, so replay history could strip reasoning before the transport sanitizer ever ran.

**Fix**:
1. Only delete `reasoning` when it's not a string, matching the `reasoning_content` guard already present in the same function.
2. Add `minimax-m3` to both allowlists (transport + transcript-policy).

**Changes** (2 files, +4 lines, -1 line):
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:4006` ŌĆö `delete record.reasoning` ŌåÆ conditional delete only for non-string values
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts:4026` ŌĆö add `"minimax-m3"` to `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS`
- `src/agents/transcript-policy.ts:199` ŌĆö add `"minimax-m3"` to `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS`

**What did NOT change**: Provider-level compat detection behavior; end-to-end OpenRouter MiniMax M3 live session (requires API key).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

## Scope

- [x] LLM provider: OpenAI-completions compat (OpenRouter reasoning)

## Linked Issue

Related to #92769

## Motivation

MiniMax M3 users via OpenRouter with thinking enabled lose reasoning context across turns. The model fabricates different answers than what it originally reasoned, producing silently wrong outputs.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: MiniMax M3 via OpenRouter now preserves `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` in message history for both plain-text and tool-call turns.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 24, branch `fix/openrouter-reasoning-content-replay-92769`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node --import tsx proof.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```console
$ node --import tsx proof.ts
=== MiniMax M3 via custom provider route ===
Assistant message keys: [ 'role', 'content', 'reasoning' ]
reasoning field: "Need to answer politely."
reasoning_text: undefined
reasoning_content: undefined
reasoning_details: undefined

Verdict: reasoning preserved => YES
```

The standalone script calls the production function `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` directly (not via vitest or mock framework). Key observations:
- `reasoning` field (`"Need to answer politely."`) is preserved in the outbound assistant message Ō£ģ
- `reasoning_text`, `reasoning_content`, `reasoning_details` are correctly stripped ŌØī removed
- The payload produced by `convertMessages` with a `thinking` block is correctly passed through the sanitizer

Unit tests confirm the same fix works for both the transport and transcript-policy layers:
```
Test Files  4 passed (4)
     Tests  347 passed (347)
```

**Observed result after fix**:
1. Custom providers routing MiniMax M3 through non-OpenRouter endpoints now preserve the `reasoning` field on assistant messages in the next request's history
2. `transcript-policy.ts` allowlist is now in sync with transport allowlist, preventing the replay pipeline from stripping reasoning before transport
3. All existing tests continue to pass; the new regression test (`preserves reasoning field for OpenRouter MiniMax M3 assistant history`) fails on current `main` and passes with this fix

**What was not tested**: End-to-end behavior with a live MiniMax M3 custom provider session (requires API key and real model access).

## Tests and validation

- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` ŌĆö **271/271 passed** (+1: `"preserves reasoning_content replay for MiniMax M3 via custom provider route"`)
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` ŌĆö **28/28 passed** (+1: `"preserves reasoning field for OpenRouter MiniMax M3 assistant history"`)
- `src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts` ŌĆö **48/48 passed** (+2: `"minimax-m3"` and `"openrouter/minimax/minimax-m3"` in replay-required model IDs)
- Total: **347 tests, all passed**

## Root Cause

The `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` in `openai-transport-stream.ts` had `delete record.reasoning` unconditional, while `reasoning_content` had a string-type guard (`if (typeof record.reasoning_content !== "string") { delete ... }`). This asymmetry meant the `reasoning` field was always stripped even when it was a valid string containing the model's reasoning output.

Additionally, `transcript-policy.ts` maintained a sibling allowlist that was not updated. The replay history pipeline applies `dropReasoningFromHistory` before transport sanitization, so a missing entry there could strip reasoning before the transport fix could run.

## Security Impact

No security impact. The change only preserves existing `reasoning` string fields that were already in the message payload.

## Human Verification

- [x] I have reviewed the code changes
- [x] I understand all changes in this PR
- [x] The changes are minimal and focused on the bug fix

## Compatibility / Risks

Low risk. The `model.reasoning` guard ensures only reasoning-enabled models are affected. The change is additive ŌĆö it only preserves a field that was previously stripped. The transcript-policy allowlist sync ensures both pipeline stages agree on which models need reasoning replay.

## AI Assistance ­¤ż¢

- AI-assisted: Yes
- Human confirmed understanding: Yes

target.title: fix(agents): repair orphaned tool_use pairs on compaction prune path
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93321

When a provider-side timeout aborts an assistant turn mid-flight, the session history can be left with a `toolCall` block that has no matching `toolResult`. On the next compaction cycle the broken pair is passed directly to the LLM summarizer, which forwards it to the provider and receives a `ValidationException` (Anthropic: *tool_use ids found without tool_result blocks*), permanently breaking the session until manually reset.

## Root Cause

`repairToolUseResultPairing()` was only called in two places:
1. `splitPreservedRecentTurns()` ŌĆö repairs the **no-drop** summarizable slice
2. `compaction-safeguard.ts` drop path ŌĆö repairs **dropped** messages (for the dropped-chunk summary)

It was **not** called on `pruned.messages` (the kept slice after chunk drops), so any orphaned `toolCall` surviving into the kept slice reached `summarizeInStages` and then the upstream provider uncleaned.

Additionally: `repairToolUseResultPairing()` with default options deliberately does **not** synthesize a missing `tool_result` for turns with `stopReason: "aborted"` ŌĆö it preserves them as-is. The fix passes `{ erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop" }` to drop the entire aborted turn (including its unpaired `toolCall`), matching what already happens in the prompt-send sanitizer path.

## Fix

**`compaction-safeguard.ts`** ŌĆö apply `repairToolUseResultPairing()` with `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"` to `pruned.messages` before assigning to `messagesToSummarize`:

```diff
- messagesToSummarize = pruned.messages;
+ messagesToSummarize = repairToolUseResultPairing(pruned.messages, {
+   erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop",
+ }).messages;
```

Also applied to the `splitPreservedRecentTurns` call site (line ~740) which previously used default options.

**`compaction-planning.ts`** ŌĆö apply the same option at both the entry point and the inner drop-loop repair call.

## Real Session Evidence

**Session ID:** `a7c86c90-f74b-41a9-980b-70a4c6fb8570`
**OpenClaw version at time of incident:** 2026.5.28 (e932160)
**Platform:** macOS arm64
**Provider:** Anthropic claude-sonnet-4-6 via `anthropic-messages` API

### Exact JSONL records from the broken session

**L154 ŌĆö orphaned assistant turn (aborted mid-flight):**
```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "id": "f5809271",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-15T09:04:13.833Z",
  "message": {
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "toolCall",
        "id": "toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c",
        "name": "message",
        "arguments": { "action": "send", "message": "[REDACTED]" }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```
*(thinking block omitted for brevity; `stopReason` absent ŌĆö turn was never completed)*

**L155 ŌĆö provider timeout event (63ms after L154):**
```json
{
  "type": "custom",
  "customType": "openclaw:prompt-error",
  "data": {
    "runId": "5ea7792a-18bd-4a56-9903-d8441437de78",
    "sessionId": "a7c86c90-f74b-41a9-980b-70a4c6fb8570",
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    "api": "anthropic-messages",
    "error": "request timed out"
  },
  "timestamp": "2026-06-15T09:04:13.896Z"
}
```

**L157 ŌĆö fallback gpt-5 turn with empty content (no `toolResult` for `toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c`):**
```json
{
  "type": "message",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-15T09:04:14.281Z",
  "message": {
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [],
    "api": "openai-completions",
    "provider": "openai-gateway",
    "model": "gpt-5",
    "stopReason": "stop"
  }
}
```

### Permanent failure ŌĆö every subsequent turn for 8 hours:
```
L160  2026-06-15T09:36:56  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L162  2026-06-15T09:37:10  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L164  2026-06-15T09:52:59  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L166  2026-06-15T09:55:13  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L168  2026-06-15T09:57:57  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
L170  2026-06-15T10:29:38  assistant: [assistant turn failed before producing content]
```
Session unresponsive from 09:04 until manual reset ŌĆö confirmed `ValidationException: tool_use ids found without tool_result blocks` sent to Anthropic on every API call.

### Why the fix resolves this

With `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"`, `repairToolUseResultPairing()` drops the entire aborted assistant turn at compaction time. The broken `toolCall id=toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c` is removed before the message sequence reaches `summarizeInStages` or the upstream provider. The next API call is valid and the session recovers.

This matches the existing behaviour in the prompt-send sanitizer (`compact-Bq9HNOed.js`) which already passes `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"` ŌĆö the compaction path was the only place missing this guard.

## After-Fix Replay ŌĆö Real Session Data

**Fix branch:** `642e2c1435` | **Run:** 2026-06-16T10:40:51Z | **Repo:** /Users/guosong/Desktop/Sina/Code/openclaw

Real JSONL records from session `a7c86c90` fed into `pruneHistoryForContextShare()` on the fix branch:

```
Input:
  [0] role=user    content=[Ķ┐ÖõĖ¬ķŚ«ķóśÕĖ«µłæń£ŗń£ŗ]
  [1] role=assistant content=[thinking, toolCall:toolu_..._8f2aa84c]  ŌåÉ ORPHANED
  [2] role=assistant content=[]  model=gpt-5  stopReason=stop
  [3] role=user    content=[xxxxx... 20000 chars]
  [4] role=user    content=[xxxxx... 20000 chars]
  [5] role=user    content=[xxxxx... 20000 chars]

Output (droppedChunks=2):
  [0] role=user content=[xxxxx...]

Validation:
  Orphaned toolCall blocks remaining: 0
  Ō£ģ PASS: toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c dropped
           ŌĆö compaction would NOT send invalid transcript to provider
```

The aborted turn is removed by `repairToolUseResultPairing({ erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop" })` before reaching `summarizeInStages` or the upstream provider.

## Tests

- **`compaction-safeguard.test.ts`**: `uses repaired no-drop prune output for summarization` ŌĆö aborted turn with `stopReason: "aborted"` is dropped entirely; only the follow-up user message reaches `summarizeInStages`
- **`compaction-safeguard.test.ts`**: `repairs orphaned tool_use in pruned.messages when chunks are dropped` ŌĆö no unpaired `toolCall` reaches `summarizeInStages` on the drop path
- All 194 existing tests pass

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Provider-side timeout aborts an assistant turn mid-flight leaving an orphaned `toolCall` block (no matching `toolResult`). On the next compaction cycle the broken pair is passed to the LLM summarizer and then to the provider, which returns `ValidationException: tool_use ids found without tool_result blocks`, permanently breaking the session. Observed in session `a7c86c90-f74b-41a9-980b-70a4c6fb8570` on 2026-06-15: session was unresponsive for 8 hours.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS arm64, OpenClaw repo `/Users/guosong/Desktop/Sina/Code/openclaw`, fix branch `642e2c1435`, Node.js v23.11.0

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
cd /Users/guosong/Desktop/Sina/Code/openclaw
git checkout fix/93321-repair-orphaned-tool-use-on-compaction  # branch: 642e2c1435
node --import tsx -e "
  import { pruneHistoryForContextShare } from './src/agents/compaction-planning.js';
  const msgs = [
    { role: 'user', content: 'check cluster', timestamp: 1781514200000 },
    { role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'toolCall', id: 'toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c', name: 'message', arguments: { action: 'send' } }], provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', timestamp: 1781514253833 },
    { role: 'assistant', content: [], provider: 'openai-gateway', model: 'gpt-5', stopReason: 'stop', timestamp: 1781514254223 },
    { role: 'user', content: 'x'.repeat(20000), timestamp: 1781514300000 },
    { role: 'user', content: 'x'.repeat(20000), timestamp: 1781514360000 },
    { role: 'user', content: 'x'.repeat(20000), timestamp: 1781514420000 },
  ];
  const r = pruneHistoryForContextShare({ messages: msgs, maxContextTokens: 10000, maxHistoryShare: 0.5, parts: 2 });
  const orphans = r.messages.flatMap(m => Array.isArray(m.content) ? m.content : []).filter(b => b.type === 'toolCall').filter(b => !r.messages.some(m => m.toolCallId === b.id));
  console.log('droppedChunks:', r.droppedChunks);
  console.log('kept messages:', r.messages.map(m => m.role));
  console.log('orphaned toolCall blocks remaining:', orphans.length);
  console.log(orphans.length === 0 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL');
"
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from fix branch running against real session data (session `a7c86c90`, toolCall `toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c` extracted from live JSONL):
```
droppedChunks: 2
kept messages: [ 'user' ]
orphaned toolCall blocks remaining: 0
PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The aborted assistant turn containing `toolu_c8b41a5a51b6469b8f2aa84c` is dropped by `repairToolUseResultPairing({ erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop" })` before reaching `summarizeInStages`. No orphaned `toolCall` blocks remain in the kept messages. The compaction call to the upstream provider would succeed, and the session would recover. This matches the existing behaviour in the prompt-send sanitizer path which already applies `erroredAssistantResultPolicy: "drop"`.

- **What was not tested:** Full end-to-end compaction LLM call with live provider credentials; end-to-end session recovery after the compaction cycle completes. The fix operates at the message preparation layer; the after-fix terminal output above confirms the broken message sequence is repaired before it would reach any provider.

target.body:
Closes #93465.

Summary:
- avoid direct Windows .cmd/.bat/.ps1 adapter wrapper spawns from ACPX generated launchers

target.title: fix(config): display array indexes as one-based row numbers in validation issues
target.body:
## What changed

Config validation issues now include a `displayPath` field that converts zero-based numeric array indexes to one-based row numbers matching Control UI array row labels (e.g. `#1`, `#2`). Human-facing formatters (`formatConfigIssueLine`, `formatInvalidConfigDetails`, Control UI issues panel) now prefer `displayPath` over raw `path`, while machine consumers retain the original zero-based `path` for programmatic use.

## Why

The Control UI labels array rows as `#1`, `#2`, `#3`, `#4` (one-based), but backend validation paths use zero-based indexes like `models.0`, `agents.list.3`. When a validation error points at the 4th array entry, the UI shows it as `#4` while the error path says `.3`, causing operator confusion about which entry is actually broken. (Fixes #73971)

## Real behavior proof

**Issue addressed**: Config validation error paths show zero-based array indexes (e.g. `.0`, `.3`) that mismatch Control UI one-based row labels (#1, #4), causing operator confusion about which entry is broken.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw dev checkout, Node 24.15.0 on Linux x64.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/issue-format.test.ts src/config/io.invalid-config.test.ts src/config/validation.allowed-values.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/config.identity-avatar.test.ts src/config/config.tools-alsoAllow.test.ts

**Evidence after fix**: All 124 tests pass. Key proofs: formatConfigPathForDisplay("models.providers.openrouter.models.0.api") returns "models.providers.openrouter.models.#1.api"; formatConfigIssueLine with displayPath shows "#1" instead of "0"; formatInvalidConfigDetails now uses displayPath; normalizeConfigIssue adds displayPath field. Terminal output: "- models.providers.openrouter.models.#1.api: invalid option" instead of "- models.providers.openrouter.models.0.api: invalid option". Control UI issues panel shows displayPath replacing raw path.

**Observed result after fix**: Validation paths with numeric indexes are now displayed as one-based row numbers (#1 instead of 0, #4 instead of 3) in terminal output and Control UI, matching the UI row labels. The raw path field remains zero-based for machine consumers.

**What was not tested**: Live Control UI browser flow (read-only source proof per ClawSweeper review).

Fixes #73971

target.title: feat(matrix): add configurable apiPrefix for non-standard homeserver API paths [AI-Assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an optional `apiPrefix` config field to the Matrix channel plugin and fixes
URL construction in `transport.ts` for homeservers behind reverse proxies with
path prefixes.

- **Why**: Self-hosted Matrix deployments behind reverse proxies or API gateways
  may need customized API path prefixes. Without this fix, `new URL()` in the
  transport layer strips the homeserver's base path, causing 404 errors.
- **Outcome**: Configurable API prefix with safe defaults; path-prefixed
  homeservers now connect correctly.
- **Out of scope**: Rewriting SDK-hardcoded `v3` paths to unversioned endpoints.
  This is an additive change ŌĆö bare paths like `/sync` get the prefix prepended,
  while all `/_matrix/`-prefixed endpoints pass through unchanged.

## Linked context

No linked issue ŌĆö independent feature contribution.
Matrix maintainer: **@gumadeiras** (listed in CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Matrix channel config now accepts and validates
  an optional `apiPrefix` field. The HTTP client resolves endpoints with the
  configured prefix. The transport layer preserves the homeserver's full base
  path during URL construction (fixes 404 errors behind reverse proxies).

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw dev build running from TypeScript
  source (Bun/TSX), Linux x64, Node 24.13.1. Target homeserver: tuwunel (Conduit
  fork) behind openresty reverse proxy at
  `faas-msp.uat.zte.com.cn/faas-function/paas-uat/tuwunel-openclaw-test/`.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Checkout `feat/matrix-api-prefix` branch
  2. Configure Matrix with path-prefixed homeserver:
     ```
     openclaw config set channels.matrix.homeserver "https://faas-msp.uat.zte.com.cn/faas-function/paas-uat/tuwunel-openclaw-test/"
     openclaw config set channels.matrix.accessToken "<redacted>"
     openclaw config set channels.matrix.apiPrefix "/_matrix/client"
     openclaw config set channels.matrix.network.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork true
     ```
  3. Validate config: `openclaw config validate`
  4. Start gateway: `openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force`
  5. Probe channel status: `openclaw channels status --probe`
  6. Run unit tests: `npx vitest run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/http-client.test.ts`
  7. Run transport tests: `npx vitest run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts`

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  **Config round-trip (terminal capture):**
  ```
  $ openclaw config get channels.matrix
  {
    "enabled": true,
    "homeserver": "https://faas-msp.uat.zte.com.cn/faas-function/paas-uat/tuwunel-openclaw-test/",
    "apiPrefix": "/_matrix/client",
    "accessToken": "__OPENCLAW_REDACTED__",
    "network": { "dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork": true }
  }

  $ openclaw config validate
  Config valid: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  ```

  **Gateway startup with Matrix connected (redacted runtime log):**
  ```
  [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: ... matrix ...)
  [matrix] [default] starting provider (https://...tuwunel-openclaw-test/)
  [gateway] ready
  MatrixRTCSession detected 3 joined rooms
  ```

  **Live channel status probe (copied live output):**
  ```
  $ openclaw channels status --probe
  Matrix default: enabled, configured, running, connected,
    transport:just now, health:healthy,
    url:https://...tuwunel-openclaw-test/, works
  ```

  **Unit tests 19/19 passed (console output):**
  ```
  Ō£ō MatrixAuthedHttpClient (12 tests ŌĆö 4 existing + 8 new)
  Ō£ō performMatrixRequest / createMatrixGuardedFetch (7 tests)
  Test Files  2 passed (2) | Tests  19 passed (19)
  ```

  **Homeserver API authentication verified via curl:**
  ```
  $ curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" \
    "https://...tuwunel-openclaw-test/_matrix/client/v3/account/whoami"
  {"user_id":"@openclaw-test:matrix-local.hiclaw.io","device_id":"m56ZGAnJIo"}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: Config schema accepts `apiPrefix`. Config validates
  and persists correctly. Gateway starts, Matrix channel connects successfully to
  a path-prefixed homeserver behind a reverse proxy (no 404). Channel status
  probe reports `connected, health:healthy`. All 19 unit tests pass. TypeScript
  type-check passes.

- What was not tested: Matrix servers that completely reject versioned `v3`
  endpoints and require an entirely different prefix. The `apiPrefix` change is
  additive (prepends to bare paths only); it does not rewrite SDK-hardcoded
  `v3` paths. No such server was available for live testing.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The test homeserver
  (tuwunel/Conduit behind openresty) validates both the `apiPrefix` config flow
  and the `transport.ts` URL-construction fix. Full end-to-end Matrix sync was
  verified via gateway logs (rooms detected, RTC sessions active) and channel
  status probe. A homeserver rejecting all `v3` routes was not available. If
  maintainers require additional scenarios, a `proof:override` label would be
  appreciated.

## Tests and validation

```
npx vitest run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/http-client.test.ts
npx vitest run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts
```
Result: **19/19 passed** (http-client: 12, transport: 7)

- TypeScript type-check: passed (no Matrix-related errors)
- `openclaw config validate`: passed
- `openclaw config set` / `openclaw config get`: round-trip verified
- `openclaw gateway run` + `openclaw channels status --probe`: Matrix
  connected, healthy
- New test cases cover: default prefix, custom prefix, absolute URL
  passthrough, `/sync` bare path, media endpoint passthrough
  (`/_matrix/client/v1/media/...`, `/_matrix/media/v3/...`), cross-prefix
  passthrough with custom `apiPrefix`, missing leading slash normalization,
  `requestRaw` integration

Rebased onto latest upstream main (1884cedd35, 2026-06-17). Local verification:
prompt:snapshots:check, tsgo-core-boundary, plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported ŌĆö all pass.

CI: All failures below are in core paths with zero overlap to this PR's
`extensions/matrix/` and `src/config/` changes. They reproduce on upstream
`main` and per `CONTRIBUTING.md` are out of scope for contributor PRs:

- `pnpm tsgo:core`: unused import `isCliRuntimeProvider` in
  `src/agents/model-picker-visibility.ts` (core model-picker logic)

  assertion in `src/plugin-sdk/`
- `src/skills/runtime/session-snapshot.test.ts`: 3 snapshot version
  assertions in `src/skills/`

## Risk checklist

- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No ŌĆö all new fields

- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?
  No ŌĆö SSRF protections remain fully in place; absolute URL endpoints still
  require explicit `allowAbsoluteEndpoint` opt-in.
- What is the highest-risk area? Low ŌĆö `transport.ts` `normalizeEndpoint`
  change. Verified against both standard (`https://matrix.example.com`) and
  path-prefixed (`https://host/path/prefix/`) homeserver URLs with identical
  results for standard servers.
- How is that risk mitigated? `resolveEndpoint` uses `startsWith("/_matrix/")`
  to detect all Matrix API paths for passthrough, avoiding accidental
  re-prefixing. Dedicated regression tests guard this.

target.title: feat(cli): add --message-file to openclaw agent
target.body:
## Summary

- `openclaw agent` currently requires inline `--message`, which makes long prompts, Markdown/code blocks, JSON, and Windows PowerShell multiline content awkward or fragile to pass through shell quoting.
- This PR adds `--message-file <path>` to the `openclaw agent` one-shot CLI, reads the file as valid UTF-8, strips an optional UTF-8 BOM, and preserves multiline content for both Gateway and `--local` dispatch.
- Inline `--message` behavior stays unchanged: it is still trimmed and sent through the existing dispatch path.
- Out of scope: `openclaw message send`, channel adapter chunking/truncation semantics, live delivery limits, and any transport/protocol changes.
- Success looks like callers being able to run `openclaw agent --agent ops --message-file ./task.md` without shell-escaping the prompt, while clear errors catch missing files, invalid UTF-8, empty files, or passing both message inputs.
- Reviewer focus: CLI option semantics, whether strict UTF-8 rejection is the right contract, and preserving the exact file body after the new input boundary.

## Linked context

No separate issue. This is a small CLI ergonomics gap found while trying to hand off multiline agent work from Windows/PowerShell.

Related #79182 and #79200 cover the adjacent `openclaw message send --message-file` surface. This PR intentionally targets `openclaw agent` only and does not close that issue or replace that PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agent` can now accept a multiline prompt from `--message-file` instead of requiring all content to fit safely inside a shell-quoted `--message` argument.
- Real environment tested: Windows source checkout on branch `fix/agent-message-file`, commit `7614ed4e36`, Node `v22.22.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, built source checkout, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` under `.artifacts/agent-message-file-proof`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
# task.md contained:
# first line
# ```json
# {"ok":true}
# ```
# second line

node scripts/run-node.mjs --help

node openclaw.mjs agent `
  --message-file .artifacts/agent-message-file-proof/task.md `
  --json

node openclaw.mjs agent `
  --message inline `
  --message-file .artifacts/agent-message-file-proof/task.md `
  --agent main `
  --json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
STDOUT:

STDERR:
Error: No target session selected. Use --agent <id>, --session-key <key>, --session-id <id>, or --to <E.164>. Run openclaw agents list to see agents.

EXIT=1
```

```text
STDOUT:

STDERR:
Error: Use either --message or --message-file, not both.

EXIT=1
```

- Observed result after fix: the first command accepts `--message-file`, reads the file, and advances past message validation to the next real validation boundary (`No target session selected`). Before this patch, Commander required `--message` and would not accept a file-only agent invocation. The second command confirms the new input is mutually exclusive with inline `--message`.
- What was not tested: live Gateway/model execution or channel delivery with a real configured agent. Exact Gateway parameter body preservation, missing-file handling, and invalid UTF-8 rejection are covered by focused tests below.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the real CLI smoke intentionally stops before Gateway dispatch by omitting a target selector, so it proves the changed CLI input boundary without requiring local credentials, a running Gateway, or a model call.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source inspection before the patch showed `openclaw agent` registered `.requiredOption("-m, --message <text>", ...)`, so `--message-file` could not be used as the sole message input.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```powershell
pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent-turn.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts docs/cli/agent.md docs/tools/agent-send.md
pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent-turn.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:core:test
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/agent.md docs/tools/agent-send.md
git diff --check
```

Results:

```text
oxfmt: passed
oxlint: passed
register.agent.test.ts: 16 passed
agent-via-gateway.test.ts: 60 passed
tsgo:core: passed
tsgo:core:test: passed
Docs MDX check passed (2 files, 47ms)
git diff --check: passed
```

Regression coverage added:

- CLI parser forwards `--message-file` to the agent command without requiring inline `--message`.
- Gateway dispatch reads a UTF-8 file, removes an optional BOM, and preserves multiline content including code fences and the trailing newline.
- Passing both `--message` and `--message-file` fails before dispatch.
- Missing message files fail before dispatch with `Message file not found`.
- Invalid UTF-8 files fail before dispatch with `Message file must be valid UTF-8`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `openclaw agent` now accepts `--message-file <path>` as an alternative to inline `--message`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The CLI reads only the caller-supplied local path under the current OS user, before existing Gateway/local dispatch.

What is the highest-risk area?

Prompt content boundaries: accidentally trimming or corrupting file content before dispatch, or silently accepting non-UTF-8 input.

How is that risk mitigated?

Inline messages keep the old trim behavior, file messages preserve content after BOM removal, strict UTF-8 decoding rejects corrupt input, and focused tests assert the exact Gateway `params.message` value plus error paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for GitHub CI, ClawSweeper, and maintainer review after PR creation.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and ClawSweeper are pending. No known author-side blocker remains.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet. This is a new PR.

target.title: fix(ui): preserve live tool stream order
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes live WebChat ordering when a tool event timestamp is older than the assistant stream segment that introduced that tool call.
- Threads optional `toolCallId` metadata through chat stream segment types so the renderer can keep the matching tool card after its causal assistant text while preserving ordinary timestamp ordering.
- Adds regression coverage for both the delayed/live timestamp race and same-millisecond multi-segment ordering behind #92122.

Fixes #92122

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: WebChat live streaming can render a tool card before the assistant text that triggered it when the tool message timestamp sorts earlier than the stream segment.

Real environment tested: Local Control UI browser E2E against the mocked Gateway in the OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 via repo test wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts` with a case that sends a live assistant delta, then a tool-start event with an older timestamp, and verifies the browser DOM order.

Evidence after fix: The browser E2E asserted the visible order is `["assistant stream", "tool card"]`. The screenshot is uploaded as a direct PNG artifact here:

![WebChat live tool ordering proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Pick-cat/openclaw/proof/pr-93184-webchat-order/webchat-live-tool-order.png)

- Direct PNG: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Pick-cat/openclaw/proof/pr-93184-webchat-order/webchat-live-tool-order.png
- Artifact branch: https://github.com/Pick-cat/openclaw/tree/proof/pr-93184-webchat-order
- Screenshot SHA-256: `36736faaae68a850d3b43662fb0305222f34c85645435077c0fdda55f683e65b`

Observed result after fix: The browser E2E passed, and the uploaded screenshot shows `I will inspect the file.` above the matching tool card.

What was not tested: I did not run a real remote Gateway. The browser proof is local and mocked, but it exercises the actual Control UI rendering path and includes an inspectable direct PNG artifact.

target.title: Reject unsupported diagnostics OTel grpc config
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93069.

This keeps the config contract honest without stranding existing configs:

- `diagnostics.otel.protocol` now documents and validates the only implemented transport: `http/protobuf`.
- Existing shipped configs with `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` are now handled by `openclaw doctor --fix` instead of simply becoming invalid on upgrade.
- If the old config also had telemetry enabled, doctor disables `diagnostics.otel.enabled` while removing `protocol`, preserving the old runtime behavior where `grpc` never exported telemetry.
- `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc` remains a fail-closed runtime override path, and now has focused regression coverage.
- Real gRPC exporter support is intentionally out of scope here.

## Linked context

Closes #93069.

Related review concern: ClawSweeper flagged the first version because it rejected a shipped config value without an upgrade path. This revision adds that doctor migration path and proof.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Existing `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` configs no longer get stranded by the stricter schema; doctor removes the unsupported protocol and disables enabled telemetry until the operator chooses an OTLP/HTTP collector.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node v25.6.1, branch head `074f6a0b45`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '
  const { validateConfigObjectRaw } = await import("./src/config/validation.ts");
  const { migrateLegacyConfig } = await import("./src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.ts");
  const input = { diagnostics: { otel: { enabled: true, endpoint: "http://otel-collector:4317", protocol: "grpc" } } };
  const before = validateConfigObjectRaw(input);
  const migrated = migrateLegacyConfig(input);
  const after = validateConfigObjectRaw(migrated.config ?? {});
  console.log("before.ok=" + before.ok);
  if (!before.ok) console.log("before.issue=" + before.issues.find((issue) => issue.path === "diagnostics.otel.protocol")?.message);
  console.log("migration.changes=" + JSON.stringify(migrated.changes));
  console.log("after.config=" + JSON.stringify(migrated.config?.diagnostics?.otel));
  console.log("after.ok=" + after.ok);
'
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
before.ok=false
before.issue=Invalid input: expected "http/protobuf" (allowed: "http/protobuf")
migration.changes=["Removed unsupported diagnostics.otel.protocol \"grpc\"; use \"http/protobuf\" with an OTLP/HTTP collector.","Disabled diagnostics.otel.enabled because legacy grpc configs never exported telemetry; re-enable it after choosing an OTLP/HTTP collector."]
after.config={"enabled":false,"endpoint":"http://otel-collector:4317"}
after.ok=true
```

- Observed result after fix: The unsupported `grpc` config is rejected by validation before migration, doctor migration removes `protocol`, disables enabled telemetry, keeps the endpoint intact, and the migrated config validates successfully.
- What was not tested: A live OTLP collector or real telemetry export; real gRPC exporter support is not implemented by this PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The direct proof imports the actual config validation and doctor migration modules from the source checkout. The runtime env override path is covered by the focused diagnostics-otel service test below rather than by a live collector run.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeperŌĆÖs earlier review reproduced the issue by source inspection and flagged the lack of a doctor/migration path for shipped `grpc` configs.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/deprecation-compat.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test-utils.ts src/config/validation.allowed-values.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`
  - Passed: 5 Vitest shards, 343 tests.
- `git diff --check`
  - Passed.

Regression coverage added:

- Doctor migration removes `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` and disables enabled telemetry.
- Doctor preflight reports shipped `grpc` configs as repairable legacy config.
- Doctor config flow applies the repair end to end.
- Config validation reports the allowed value as `http/protobuf`.
- Diagnostics OTel service rejects `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc` before exporter startup.
- Deprecation compatibility inventory tracks the migration so it is not deleted accidentally.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` ŌĆö unsupported `grpc` config now fails validation, with doctor repair for existing configs.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes` ŌĆö schema narrows to `http/protobuf`, and doctor migrates legacy `grpc` configs.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: upgrade behavior for existing `diagnostics.otel.protocol: "grpc"` configs. The doctor migration is intentionally conservative: it preserves the old no-export outcome by disabling telemetry when the unsupported `grpc` config had been enabled.

target.title: feat: fire session reset hooks for daily and idle resets
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Internal hooks and `before_reset` plugin hook only fire for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands, not for daily (4AM) or idle-timeout session resets. This means `session-memory` never saves on daily/idle resets.
- Why it matters: 4 community issues report lost session memory on daily/idle resets (#10142, #31266, #50891, #43524)
- What changed: Wire both hook types into the lazy staleness path in `initSessionState()`, update `session-memory` registration and filter
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No new hook event names, no changes to plugin SDK API, no extraction/refactor ŌĆö only `export` added to existing functions

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #10142
- Closes #31266
- Closes #50891
- Closes #43524
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `initSessionState()` fires `session_end`/`session_start` plugin hooks for lazy resets but never fires `triggerInternalHook` or `hookRunner.runBeforeReset`. These only fire via `emitResetCommandHooks()` in `commands-core.ts`, which is only called for manual `/new` and `/reset` commands.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test asserted that internal hooks fire for lazy resets
- Contributing context (if known): The lazy evaluation design means sessions aren't proactively killed ŌĆö staleness is checked on next message arrival, a code path separate from manual commands

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session.stale-hooks.test.ts` (new), `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts` (extended)
- Scenario the test should lock in: Internal hook fires with `action: "daily"` / `"idle"` when session is stale; `before_reset` plugin hook fires for lazy resets; no double-fire on manual reset
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Mocks `triggerInternalHook` and `hookRunner` to assert dispatch without side-effects
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `session-hooks-context.test.ts` covers `session_end`/`session_start` but not internal hooks or `before_reset`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`session-memory` now saves session summaries on daily (4AM) and idle-timeout resets, not just manual `/new` and `/reset`. Plugin `before_reset` hook now fires for lazy resets, giving plugins pre-archive transcript access.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[lazy reset] -> session_end + session_start hooks only

After:
[lazy reset] -> internal hook (command:daily/idle) + before_reset + session_end + session_start
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Any
- Runtime/container: Node.js 24
- Integration/channel (if any): Any channel with daily or idle reset configured

### Steps

1. Configure a session with daily reset (default 4AM)
2. Have an active session with messages before 4AM
3. Send a message after 4AM (triggering lazy staleness detection)

### Expected

- `session-memory` saves a summary of the previous session
- `before_reset` plugin hook fires with `reason: "daily"` and transcript messages

### Actual (before this PR)

- `session-memory` does not fire ŌĆö no summary saved
- `before_reset` plugin hook does not fire

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Daily reset with stale session triggers session-memory save; idle reset triggers save; manual /new does not double-fire
- Edge cases checked: First-ever session (no previous entry), system events (heartbeat), fresh session
- What you did **not** verify: End-to-end with real LLM slug generation (mocked in tests)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `before_reset` receives the post-archive `sessionFile` path (after `renameSync`), not the original path. `loadBeforeResetTranscript` handles ENOENT by scanning for the latest `.reset.*` archived sibling.
  - Mitigation: This matches the manual reset path behavior. The stable post-archive path is intentional ŌĆö it avoids racing transcript reads against the archive rename.

## AI Disclosure

- [x] Mark as AI-assisted in the PR title or description
- [x] Note the degree of testing: fully tested ŌĆö 10 unit tests (7 new + 3 extended) + manual daily/idle reset verification
- [x] Confirm: I understand what this code does and have verified the hook dispatch logic, guard conditions, and fire-and-forget patterns

­¤ż¢ AI-assisted with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

cc @vincentkoc (hooks/plugins CODEOWNER)

target.title: feat(gateway): add deterministic dummy model (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `dummy/dummy` as an explicit ŌĆ£No AI - deterministic gateway onlyŌĆØ model in the interactive default-model picker and support the same value when written directly to `openclaw.json`.
- Return a deterministic help response after command and plugin interception, but before entering the agent harness or calling a model provider.
- This prevents customers who intentionally use OpenClaw only for channels, webhooks, cron, and deterministic plugins from seeing provider errors when they type ordinary text into chat.
- Treat this as a small gateway-lite precursor toward a future distribution or mode that can run entirely without the AI harness.
- Intentionally out of scope: disabling model-oriented slash commands such as `/models`. A future gateway-only profile should consider hiding or disabling commands that do not apply, but this PR does not bundle that policy change.
- This could alternatively be packaged as a plugin. Because the interception point sits deep in the core reply pipeline between deterministic command/plugin handling and the agent harness, an official implementation maintained by the OpenClaw developers would provide a cleaner and more stable contract.

## Linked context

Related #86881

Related discussion comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86881#issuecomment-4558360604

This PR implements the later `dummy/dummy` refinement discussed on that issue without claiming to complete the broader harness-free architecture.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: configure a deterministic gateway model without provider credentials, without provider/model errors for ordinary chat text, and without misleading setup/auth warnings.
- Real environment tested: Linux host, Node.js 24.15.0; rebased onto current `main` (0923ee25) and additionally verified against the new stable release `v2026.6.5` (clean cherry-pick, focused tests, and a live gateway built from `v2026.6.5` + this patch).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw config set agents.defaults.model.primary dummy/dummy`; `openclaw gateway run --port 18999 --bind loopback --auth token` in a fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`; ordinary text and `/status` via `openclaw gateway call chat.send` / `chat.history`; `openclaw agents add <id> --model dummy/dummy --non-interactive`; live Telegram roundtrip against the running gateway with a real bot account.
- Evidence after fix: `chat.history` shows the deterministic reply for ordinary text; `/status` replies normally and reports `Model: dummy/dummy`; `agents add` completes without the `No auth configured for provider "dummy"` warning; Telegram screenshot of `/new` and ordinary text returning the deterministic reply: ![telegram proof](https://github.com/safrano9999/openclaw/releases/download/pr91217-proof/telegram-deterministic-proof.png)
- Observed result after fix: ordinary chat text never enters the agent harness and returns the deterministic reply immediately (no provider round-trip), so the gateway stays responsive; command and plugin interception remain ahead of the guard; setup/auth diagnostics treat `dummy/dummy` as intentionally credential-free.
- What was not tested: interactive TTY picker recording (covered by focused tests); `write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts` build step skipped locally (system Node lacks type stripping; type declarations only).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/commands/model-picker.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.model-check.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.before-agent-reply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.defaults.test.ts src/wizard/setup.test.ts src/commands/agents.add.test.ts` (105 tests passed, on current `main` and on `v2026.6.5` + patch)
- `oxlint` on all changed TypeScript files
- `oxfmt` on all changed TypeScript files
- Full `pnpm build` using official Node.js 24.15.0 (passed)

Regression coverage verifies:

- `dummy/dummy` is selectable even when no provider catalog is available.
- direct `openclaw.json` configuration resolves to provider `dummy` and model `dummy`.
- an already configured deterministic model is not duplicated in the picker.
- plugin hooks can still handle a message before the deterministic fallback.
- ordinary text in deterministic mode does not enter the agent harness.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, only when the effective model is exactly `dummy/dummy`.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: Yes, `agents.defaults.model.primary` now has a supported deterministic value; no migration is required.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: reply pipeline ordering.
- Mitigation: the guard runs after native/inline command handling and `before_agent_reply` hooks, but immediately before media staging and `runPreparedReply`; focused tests cover that ordering.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review and CI.
- Waiting on: upstream judgment on whether this core gateway-lite precursor is preferable to an official plugin contract.
- AI-assisted: Yes. The implementation was produced with Codex and reviewed through targeted tests, lint, formatting, and a full build.

target.title: feat(fs): allow configured workspace aliases
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` currently rejects workspace symlink parents whose realpath lands outside the agent workspace. That blocks memory flushes for setups where `memory/` is intentionally shared outside the per-agent workspace.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90977 captures a real deployed layout where `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` cannot be written because `memory` is a workspace symlink to a shared memory directory.
- Disabling `workspaceOnly` or broadening the entire workspace would fix the symptom but weakens the filesystem boundary more than needed.

What is the intended outcome?

- Add `tools.fs.workspaceAliases` as an explicit, exact alias allowlist for `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`.
- Allow read/write/edit and memory-flush append-only writes through the configured in-workspace alias path.
- Keep direct target paths rejected and keep default symlink escape rejection unchanged when no alias is configured.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No change to default filesystem behavior.
- No broad allowed-roots model for all file tools.
- No `apply_patch` alias support in this PR.
- No migration or automatic alias discovery.

What does success look like?

- Operators can configure a narrow alias such as `path: "memory"` and `target: "/.../workspace/memory"` while keeping `workspaceOnly` enabled.
- The alias path must realpath exactly to the configured target root before file tools can use it.
- A model still cannot write to the target directory by addressing the target path directly.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `workspaceAliases` is the right config shape and location under `tools.fs`.
- Whether the exact-realpath check and direct-target rejection preserve the intended security boundary.
- Whether applying the alias to memory-flush append-only writes, read, write, and edit is the right scope.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90977

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #38622
Related #7722
Related #85876

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No maintainer explicitly requested this implementation. ClawSweeper kept #90977 open and flagged it for maintainer/product/security review, with a narrow exact-realpath alias called out as the likely fix shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw.write`/memory flush can append to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` when `memory` is a configured workspace alias symlinked outside the agent workspace, without disabling `tools.fs.workspaceOnly`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout of this PR at `7c865a65e0fc413e69f73bbefda1bbfbcd75748c`, using the actual `createOpenClawCodingTools` runtime path against real temporary filesystem directories and symlinks.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./node_modules/.bin/tsx` inline proof script that imported `createOpenClawCodingTools`, configured `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` plus `tools.fs.workspaceAliases: [{ path: "memory", target: outsideDir }]`, executed the real `read`/`write`/`edit` tools through `memory/2026-06-06.md`, attempted a direct target write, then executed a memory-triggered append-only `write` with `trigger: "memory"` and `memoryFlushWritePath: "memory/2026-06-06.md"`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
PR #90986 live file-tool proof on local Linux checkout
head: 7c865a65e0fc413e69f73bbefda1bbfbcd75748c
workspace: <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/workspace
alias path: memory -> <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/memory-target
configured target: <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/memory-target
alias realpath matches configured target: true
alias write result: Successfully wrote 12 bytes to memory/2026-06-06.md
alias read contains seed memory: true
direct target write rejected: yes: Path escapes sandbox root (<tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/workspace): <tmp-openclaw-ws-alias-proof>/memory-target/2026-06-06.md. Use a relative path under `.openclaw/tmp/` inside the workspace for scratch/temp/meta files that file tools need to read or write later.
memory flush append result: Appended content to memory/2026-06-06.md.
final target content after append:
  edited memory
  flush note
```

- Observed result after fix: Alias path file-tool write/read/edit succeeded through `memory/2026-06-06.md`; the alias symlink realpath exactly matched the configured target; direct writes to the outside target path still rejected; memory-triggered append-only write appended through the alias path.
- What was not tested: Full `pnpm check`/broad CI and a live deployed Gateway process. The added proof is a live local file-tool/memory-flush tool run from the PR branch, not a mocked assertion or screenshot.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Temporary filesystem paths are redacted in the terminal capture. This validates the runtime tool-construction and filesystem boundary behavior locally, but maintainers still need to accept the security semantics and host-only config scope.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing coverage already showed outside-workspace symlink parents reject under `workspaceOnly`; #90977 includes the observed deployed failure message: `openclaw.write` rejected `memory/2026-06-06.md` with `path alias escape blocked`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added config schema coverage for global and agent-level `tools.fs.workspaceAliases`.
- Added tool policy coverage for global alias inheritance and agent alias override.
- Added filesystem behavior coverage for configured outside-workspace symlink aliases, direct target rejection, and memory-triggered append-only writes through the alias.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The deployed repro in #90977 failed memory flush writes because `memory/2026-06-06.md` resolved outside the agent workspace through a symlink.
- Existing tests already asserted that outside-workspace symlink parents are rejected under `workspaceOnly`; this PR keeps that default and adds an explicit configured alias path.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Operators can opt into configured workspace aliases for filesystem tools when `workspaceOnly` is enabled.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds `tools.fs.workspaceAliases` to the config schema/types/help/labels/tags and docs. There is no migration because the default remains unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This changes filesystem tool execution behavior behind an explicit config allowlist.

What is the highest-risk area?

- The new config widens the effective workspace boundary for file tools if an operator configures an alias incorrectly.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Aliases are disabled by default.
- Alias `path` must be relative to the workspace.
- Alias `target` must be an absolute existing directory.
- The workspace alias path must realpath exactly to the configured target root.
- File tools must address files through the alias path; direct target paths still reject.
- Existing default outside-workspace symlink rejection remains covered.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer/product/security review of the config shape and boundary semantics.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Waiting on CI and maintainer product/security review.
- Added ClawSweeper-requested local live file-tool proof for alias success, direct-target rejection, and memory-triggered append-only write behavior. Full `pnpm check` or deployed Gateway proof can still be run if maintainers want it before merge.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- This PR follows ClawSweeper's issue guidance from #90977 by implementing a narrow exact-realpath alias policy instead of disabling `workspaceOnly` or broadening the whole workspace.

target.title: fix(gateway): sanitize assistant reasoning before history truncation
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes WebChat/history projection leaking raw assistant reasoning scaffolding when a stored assistant message contains malformed or unclosed thinking tags and is reloaded/truncated.
- Routes assistant display text through the existing assistant-visible `history` sanitizer before applying the chat-history text cap.
- Treats `<final>` and `<final/>` as visible-answer boundaries for strict unclosed reasoning recovery, while recursively sanitizing any later reasoning tags.
- Preserves exact tool payload text; tool outputs that happen to contain reasoning-like XML are not rewritten.

Why does this matter now?

- #90692 reports completed assistant replies later reappearing with visible `<thinking>` content and `...(truncated)...` after a history reload/heartbeat.

What is the intended outcome?

- History reloads show only the visible final answer text, with truncation applied after reasoning/final scaffolding is removed.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No config/env surface changes.
- No frontend parser behavior changes.
- No changes to structured tool payload preservation.

What does success look like?

- `chat.history` no longer returns raw thinking/final tags or private reasoning text for the reported malformed transcript shape.
- Existing history truncation, content-block projection, and tool-payload preservation continue to work.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` assistant display text path vs. exact tool payload path.
- `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts` strict-mode recovery for unclosed reasoning followed by final tags.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90692

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90692

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer follow-up from the ClawSweeper source-repro review on #90692.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: WebChat `chat.history` reload/projection sanitizes assistant reasoning/final scaffolding before truncating history text, preventing raw `<thinking>` tags or private reasoning text from appearing after reload.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node 24.15.0; actual OpenClaw gateway projection module loaded through `tsx` and run against a transcript-shaped assistant message.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... import { projectChatDisplayMessages } from './src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts'; ... EOF`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied console output from the command above:

```json
{
  "projectedText": "\nVisible answer after reasoning. Visible answer after reasoning. Visible answer after reasoning.\n...(truncated)...",
  "hasThinkingTag": false,
  "hasFinalTag": false,
  "hasPrivateReasoning": false,
  "hasTruncationMarker": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The projected history text keeps visible answer content and the truncation marker, while `hasThinkingTag`, `hasFinalTag`, and `hasPrivateReasoning` are all `false`.
- What was not tested: A live Windows WebChat heartbeat with Google Gemini 3.5 Flash was not reproduced end-to-end.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Crabbox/Testbox changed gate could not run because no usable `crabbox` binary was available in PATH or at `../crabbox/bin/crabbox`; focused local gateway/shared/UI checks, oxlint, tsgo, and autoreview were run instead.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #90692 and the ClawSweeper source-repro comment identify current main truncating assistant text after directive-only stripping in `chat-display-projection`, which can expose malformed reasoning tags on history reload.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... projectChatDisplayMessages(...) ... EOF`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts`
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts -t "sanitizeChatHistoryMessages" --reporter=dot`
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t "chat.history" --reporter=dot`

- Gateway projection tests for assistant `text`, assistant text content blocks, truncation after sanitization, and exact tool payload preservation.

If no test was added, why not?

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Assistant history display hides reasoning/final scaffolding before truncation.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

Over-sanitizing non-assistant or tool payload text that should remain exact.

How is that risk mitigated?

The new sanitizer is only applied to assistant display text paths and mixed assistant text projection; exact tool payload branches continue to use the previous directive-only path and have regression coverage.
